entry.title: Online Camera LiDAR Fusion and Object Detection on Hybrid Data for Autonomous Driving
entry.content: Online Camera LiDAR Fusion and Object Detection on Hybrid Data for Autonomous DrivingAbstract Improve the LiDAR and camera fusion (Extrinct Calibration) Intensity discontinuities and erosion and dilation of the edge image for increased robustness against shadows and visual patterns, which is a recurring problem in point cloud related work use a gradient-free optimizer Fusion pipeline is lightweight and able to run in real-time Modify from Faster R-CNN Test dataset KITTI data set Outlook on how radar can be added to the fusion pipeline via velocity matching real-time with 10Hz ComparisionSensors RGB cameras: color and texture information good for object classification limited detection range perform poorly in limited lighting or adverse weather conditions cheap LiDARs provide precise distance info wider range detection small object detection no colar info performance decreases in heavy rain expensive Radars provide precise distance and velocity info work well in inclement weather rather low resolution (и§ЈжһҗеәҰ) bad object detection Fusion techniques High-level fusion (HLF) Centralized System detects objects with each sensor separately subsequently combines these detections limited available information Low-level fusion (LLF) Distributed System Combines all the data from raw data level More difficulties combining the data Key of Sensing Time-synchronized for ego-motion Fusion and detection algorithms need to be real-time Related Work Use 3D markers пјҲinconvenientпјүREF Deep learning based end-to-end architecture for feature extraction, feature matching and global regression REF Large amount of data is required for training Separate data collection for each vehicle Edge alignments between optical camera and LiDAR data using reflectivity values REF Uses an exhaustive grid search to fit edges in image and point cloud data takes to many time вҖ“> not real-time (The One this paper applies)Target-less camera LiDAR calibration REF Methodology Use LLF prevent the occurrence of aberrations and duplicate objects Use MLF An abstraction sitting on top of LLF, where extracted features from multiple sensor data are fused Sensor Velodyne HDL-64E S3 Fusion of Lidar and Camera (extrinct calibration) Relies on accurate instrict calibration of sensors Use a ring pattern Calibration board REF Reduce 70% reduction of the average reproject errors Finding the 4x4 transformation matrix TL TL cconsists of a rotation and a translation (6 degrees) Find edges in camara images match in point cloud with similarity function S Use the inverse distance transformation (IDT) and erosion and dilation (ED) to increase the robustness to shadows in the scen

entry.title: 3Dж„ҹжё¬жҠҖиЎ“
entry.content: 3Dж„ҹжё¬жҠҖиЎ“3Dж„ҹжё¬жҠҖиЎ“зӣ®еүҚдё»иҰҒеҸҜеҲҶзӮәз«Ӣй«”иҰ–иҰәпјҲStereo VisionпјүгҖҒзөҗж§Ӣе…үпјҲStructured LightпјүгҖҒйЈӣжҷӮжё¬и·қпјҲTime of Flightпјӣз°ЎзЁұпјҡTOF з«Ӣй«”иҰ–иҰәпјҲStereo Visionпјүз«Ӣй«”иҰ–иҰәпјҲStereo VisionпјүжҳҜеҹәж–јдәәзңјиҰ–е·®зҡ„еҺҹзҗҶпјҢеңЁиҮӘз„¶е…үжәҗдёӢпјҲиў«еӢ•ејҸе…үжәҗпјүпјҢйҖҸйҒҺе…©еҖӢжҲ–е…©еҖӢд»ҘдёҠзӣёж©ҹжЁЎзө„еҫһдёҚеҗҢзҡ„и§’еәҰе°ҚеҗҢдёҖзү©й«”жӢҚж”қеҪұеғҸпјҢеҶҚйҖІиЎҢдёүи§’жё¬йҮҸжі•зӯүйҒӢз®—дҫҶеҸ–еҫ—иҲҮзү©й«”д№Ӣй–“зҡ„и·қйӣўиіҮиЁҠгҖӮ е„Әй»һпјҡ зңҒйӣ» жҳҺдә®з’°еўғиЎЁзҸҫдҪіпјҢйҒ©еҗҲеңЁжҲ¶еӨ–й•·жңҹйҒӢдҪң дҪҺжҲҗжң¬ зјәй»һпјҡ и»ҹй«”жј”з®—жі•иӨҮйӣң е»¶йҒІжҖ§й«ҳ дёҚйҒ©еҗҲеңЁжҳҸжҡ—з’°еўғдҪҝз”Ё жҮүз”Ёпјҡз«Ӣй«”иҰ–иҰәзӣ®еүҚе·ІжҮүз”ЁеңЁеҗ„зЁ®3Dзӣёж©ҹгҖҒAR/VRй ӯжҲҙејҸиЈқзҪ®гҖҒж©ҹеҷЁдәәзӯүй ҳеҹҹпјҢйҖҡеёёиҲҮзөҗж§Ӣе…үгҖҒTOFж··еҗҲдҪҝз”ЁпјҢеҰӮжҷәж…§и»ҠжҮүз”Ёй ҳеҹҹгҖӮ зөҗж§Ӣе…үпјҲStructured Lightпјүзөҗж§Ӣе…үпјҲStructured LightпјүжҳҜдёҖзЁ®дё»еӢ•ејҸж·ұеәҰж„ҹжё¬жҠҖиЎ“пјҢеҹәжң¬йӣ¶зө„д»¶еҢ…еҗ«IRзҷје°„еҷЁгҖҒIRзӣёж©ҹжЁЎзө„гҖҒRGBзӣёж©ҹжЁЎзө„зӯү е…¶еҺҹзҗҶжҳҜйҒҠзҙ…еӨ–з·ҡжҠ•еҪұж©ҹе…Ҳе°Қзү©й«”зҷје°„зү№е®ҡең–жЎҲзҡ„е…үж–‘пјҲPatternпјүпјҢеҶҚз¶“з”ұж”қеҪұж©ҹдҫҶжҺҘж”¶зү©й«”иЎЁйқўдёҠзҡ„е…үж–‘ең–жЎҲз·ЁзўјпјҲLight CodingпјүпјҢйҖІиҖҢжҜ”е°ҚиҲҮеҺҹе§ӢжҠ•е°„е…үж–‘зҡ„з•°еҗҢпјҢдёҰеҲ©з”Ёдёүи§’еҺҹзҗҶиЁҲз®—еҮәзү©й«”зҡ„дёүз¶ӯеә§жЁҷгҖӮ е„Әй»һпјҡ ж·ұеәҰжә–зўәй«ҳ зјәй»һпјҡ жҳ“еҸ—иҮӘз„¶е…үз…§еҪұйҹҝ ијғйҒ©еҗҲзҹӯи·қйӣўжё¬йҮҸ жҮүз”Ёпјҡзӣ®еүҚе·ІжҮүз”Ёж–јдәәиҮүиҫЁиӯҳгҖҒй«”ж„ҹйҒҠжҲІж©ҹгҖҒе·ҘжҘӯз”Ёж©ҹеҷЁиҰ–иҰәжӘўжё¬пјҲAOIпјүзӯүй ҳеҹҹ йЈӣиЎҢжҷӮй–“жё¬и·қпјҲTime of Flightпјӣз°ЎзЁұпјҡTOFпјүTOFд№ҹжҳҜдёҖзЁ®дё»еӢ•ејҸж·ұеәҰж„ҹжё¬жҠҖиЎ“пјҢе…¶еҺҹзҗҶзӮәйҖҸйҒҺзҙ…еӨ–з·ҡжҠҳиҝ”зҡ„жҷӮй–“еҺ»иЁҲз®—и·ҹзү©й«”д№Ӣй–“зҡ„и·қйӣўпјҢд»Ҙеҫ—еҮә3DжҷҜж·ұең–гҖӮеҹәжң¬йӣ¶зө„д»¶еҢ…жӢ¬IRзҷје°„еҷЁгҖҒIRжҺҘж”¶еҷЁгҖҒRGBзӣёж©ҹжЁЎзө„е’Ңж„ҹе…үе…ғд»¶жҲ–ж„ҹжҮүйҷЈеҲ—гҖӮдё»иҰҒеҲҶзӮәiTOF & dTOFе…©зЁ®йЎһеҲҘ iTOF(indirect-ToF)iToF жҳҜеҲ©з”Ёзҷје°„зү№е®ҡй »зҺҮзҡ„е…үпјҢжӘўжё¬еҸҚе°„е…үе’Ңзҷје°„еҷЁд№Ӣй–“зҡ„дҪҚзҪ®е·®и·қ(з®—зӣёдҪҚе·®)пјҢжё¬йҮҸйЈӣиЎҢжҷӮй–“ е„Әй»һпјҡ ијғдҪҺжҲҗжң¬ ијғй«ҳи§ЈжһҗеәҰе„ӘеӢўзјәй»һпјҡ йҒ©з”Ёж–јзҹӯи·қйӣўжё¬и·қ пјҲз’°еўғе…үжәҗжңғе°Қйӣ»и·Ҝз”ўз”ҹе№Іж“ҫпјү жҮүз”Ёпјҡдё»иҰҒжҮүз”ЁеңЁжүӢж©ҹиҮүйғЁиҫЁиӯҳгҖҒжүӢеӢўиҫЁиӯҳзӯүжё¬и·қи·қйӣўијғзҹӯзҡ„е ҙжҷҜдёӯ dTOF (direct-ToF)dToF жҳҜйҖҸйҒҺзӣҙжҺҘеҗ‘жё¬йҮҸзү©й«”зҷје°„йӣ·е°„и„ҲиЎқпјҢдёҰжё¬йҮҸеҸҚе°„и„ҲиЎқе’Ңзҷје°„еҷЁд№Ӣй–“зҡ„жҷӮй–“й–“йҡ”пјҢеҫ—еҲ°е…үзҡ„йЈӣиЎҢжҷӮй–“иҖҢзӣҙжҺҘиЁҲз®—еҫ…жё¬зү©й«”зҡ„и·қйӣўгҖӮеёёиҰӢзҡ„ dToF ж„ҹжё¬еҷЁжҺҘж”¶з«ҜжҳҜдҪҝз”Ёе–®е…үеӯҗеҙ©жҪ°дәҢжҘөй«” (SPAD)пјҢжҲ–зҙҜеҙ©ејҸе…үйӣ»дәҢжҘөй«” (APD) е„Әй»һпјҡ еңЁеӨҡеҖӢзү©д»¶зҡ„ж„ҹжё¬дёҠзІҫжә–еәҰијғй«ҳ жӣҙйҒ©з”Ёж–јијғйҒ и·қзҡ„е ҙжҷҜзјәй»һпјҡ dToF зӣёијғж–ј iToF дҫҶиӘӘиЈҪдҪңеӣ°йӣЈеәҰиҰҒй«ҳдёҠдёҚе°‘(йңҖиҰҒијғеӨҡsensor) жҲҗжң¬ијғй«ҳ жҮүз”ЁпјҡжҮүз”ЁеңЁVR/ARе’ҢиҮӘй§•и»Ҡе ҙжҷҜ дёүеӨ§ж„ҹжё¬жҜ”ијғ

entry.title: Passiv
entry.content: Passiv Es gibt Verben, die keine Passivform bilden kГ¶nnen. Dazu gehГ¶ren alle Verben ohne Akkusativobjekt, also zum Beispiel reflexive Verben (z. B. sich schneiden) oder Verben, die im Perfekt mit sein gebildet werden (z. B. rennen). Vorgangspassiv Mit dem Vorgangspassiv (werden-Passiv) betonst du eine Handlung oder einen Vorgang: Was passiert? пјҲз”ЁдҫҶжҸҸиҝ°зҷјз”ҹзҡ„еӢ•дҪңпјү Form: Werden + Partizip II Beispiel: Ein Mann wurde angefahren. Dem Verletzten wurde ein Verband angelegt. Jetzt wird der Mann ins Krankenhaus gebracht. Zeitstufen Zustandspassiv Mit dem Zustandspassiv beschreiben wir den Zustand nach einer Handlung. пјҲз”ЁдҫҶиЎЁзӨәеӢ•дҪңе®ҢеҫҢзҡ„зӢҖж…Ӣ) Form: Sein+ Partizip II Beispiel: Er ist verletzt. Die TГјr ist geГ¶ffnet. Zeitstufen PassiversatzPassiv mit Modalverben Passiv mit kГ¶nnen (sollen/mГјssen) = sein + zu + Infinitiv Beispiel: Die Experimente sind bereits von Kindergartenkindern durchzufГјhren. Passiv mit kГ¶nnen = sich lassen + Infinitiv Beispiel: Die Experimente lassen sich bereits von Kindergartenkindern durchzufГјhren. Passiv mit kГ¶nnen = sein + AdjektivпјҲmit Endung -bar/lichпјү Beispiel: Die Experimente sind bereits von Kindergartenkindern durchzufГјhrbar.

entry.title: Container
entry.content: ContainerOutline Virtualization: machine level Virtualization: OS level FreeBSD jail Docker Virtualization вҖ“ machine level - Virtual machine Hardware virtualization Emulate CPU, RAM, HDD, Network Interface Host OS and Guest OS Isolated between each guest OS Hypervisor (KVM) QEMU, VirtualBox, VMWareвҖҰ EC2, GCE Google Computed Engine Intel VT-x and AMD-V CPU instructions to improve performance of virtualization CPU and I/O virtualization Pros Better use of IT resources Elasticity Cons worse performance Virtualization вҖ“ OS level - Containers Multiple isolated user space instances Shared Kernal use the same operating system as the host one Containers can be live migrated (without restart service) Technologies Docker вҖ“ works on Windows, Mac OS X, and Linux as host OS Jails вҖ“ works on FreeBSD LXC, OpenVZ Pros Better performance Better Security Use the same environment in development and production Cons Complex to understand FreeBSD jail jail(8) Detail of jail DockerIntroduction Most popular OS level virtualization technology in the 2010s Use different interfaces to access virtualization features of Linux kernel Open sourced Infrastructure as Code Dockerfile Reuse pre-built images Automate the process of building environment Image Layer A writeable layer on top of a bunch of read-only layers Each RUN has its own commit Keep image as small as possible overlayfs combining numerous directories into one directory Command Lines docker pull Pull an image from public or private repository docker build Build the image from Dockerfile docker run Run an instance docker kill Kill an instance docker rm Remove resource used by an instance docker ps Show all instance docker push Push the image to docker repository Pros and Cons Pros Infrastructure as Code (IaC) simplify the operating effort Cons Security Scalability

entry.title: File System
entry.content: File SystemFiles ll or ls -l File System Architecture Application Kernel Hardware Applications call system-calls to request service Kernel invokes corresponding drivers Purpose of filesystem Represent and organize the systemвҖҷs storage Four main components: Namespace Naming things and arranging in a hierarchy API System calls for navigating and manipulating nodes Security model Implementation API вҖ“ System Call вҖ“ OS Relationship Objects in the filesystem: Files and directories Hardware device files Processes information Pathname Absolute path(start with /) Relative path(start from the current directory) Layout of File Systems hier(7) Mounting file system mount(8) вҖңmountвҖқ command Map the mount point of the existing file tree to the root of the newly attached filesystem The previous contents of the mount point become inaccessible mountд»ҘеҫҢ,еғҸжҳҜд»»ж„Ҹй–ҖйҖҡеҲ°еҸҰдёҖеҖӢдё–з•ҢпјҢеҺҹжң¬й–ҖеҫҢзҡ„з©әй–“е°ұжңғзңӢдёҚеҲ°пјҢunmountеҫҢжүҚеҸҜзңӢеҲ°еҺҹдҫҶй–ҖеҫҢзҡ„з©әй–“ Filesystem table вҖ“ fstab Automatically mounted at boot time /etc/fstab Filesystem in this file will be checked and mounted automatically when booting umount(8) вҖңumountвҖқ command Busy filesystem SomeoneвҖҷs current directory is there or there are opened files Use вҖңumount -fвҖқ We can use lsof or fstat like utilities to figure out who makes it busy File Types Directory . and .. mkdir / rmdir UNIX domain socket Created by socket() Be referenced through a filesystem object rather than a network port Symbolic Link A file which points to another pathname ln -s ori-file soft-file` Like вҖңshort-cutвҖқ in WindowsInode and File inode A structure that records information of a file ls -i to see each fileвҖҷs inode number Hard Link V.S. Symbolic Link Hard link Associate two or more filenames with the same inode Must in the same partition ln ori-file hard-file Soft (symbolic) link A file which points to another pathname ln -s ori-file soft-file File Access Mode rwx r-x r-x User, group, other privileges chmod command change access authorities chmod(1) setuid, setgid, sticky bit setuid, setgid on file The effective uid/gid of resulting process will be set to the UID/GID of the file setgid on directory Cause newly created files within the directory to be the same group as directory sticky on directory Do not allow to delete or rename a file unless you are вҖ“ The owner of the file вҖ“ The owner of the directory вҖ“ root Changing File Owner Commands: chown вҖ“ change user owner chgrp вҖ“ change group owner

entry.title: Periodic Processes
entry.content: Periodic ProcessesGoal: Do things at right time automaticallyCron daemon: The daemon that handles periodic execution Cron daemon reads configuration file and executes commands ontime Details: Look up man cron(8), crontab(1), crontab(5)Configuration file crontab Location of user cron configuration file ![](https://i.imgur.com/8EdQzPs.png =400x200) Location of System Cron Configuration file /etc/crontab Configuration File Format Rules: *matches everything Single character matches exactly Dash(-) matches range Comma(,) matches any listed value Slash(/) matches skips of the numberвҖҷs value through the range Examples: Special Sting or Time crontab Command crontab -e [-u user] Edit the [userвҖҷs] crontab using editor crontab -l List the content of the crontab crontab -r Remove the current crontab crontab Management To Allow or deny user from using cron daemon By default, all users can have their own crontab log System crontab: /etc/crontab Periodic Utility Run periodic system function under /etc/periodic /etc/periodic.conf For custom system programs: /usr/local/etc/periodic Execution order depends on filenames Use number as prefix to control the order /etc/periodic.conf

entry.title: FTP
entry.content: FTPIntrodution to FTP File Transfer Protocol Used to transfer data from one computer to another over the internet Client-Server Architecture Separated control/data connections Control connection: Created when an FTP session is established Only for Control info Data connection: Each time that data is sent, a distinct TCP data connect is established Security concern & Solutions Need encryption for both Control and Data connection S1:FTP over SSH A normal FTP session tunneled through a SSH channel S2:SSH File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) Both commands and data are encrypted while transmitting One Connection and poor performance S3:FTP over TLS (ftps, ftpes) Only commands are encrypted while transmitting Better performance Pure-FTBdIntroduction A small, easy to set up, fast and secure FTP server Support chroot FTP over TLS Virtual Users, and Unix authentication Restrictions on clients, and system-wideInstallation Use port or pkg install in Freebsd Startup Add pureftpd_enable="YES" in /etc/rc.conf Configurations File: /usr/local/etc/pure-ftpd.conf Tools and Commands pure-* pure-ftpwho List info of users who are currently connecting to the FTP server. pure-pw Manage Virtual Users in PureDB format Real Implimented Products FileZilla A graphical cross-platform FTP client Support TLS Pure-FTPd WebUI PHP based web interface for Pure-FTPdImplimentation Reference FTP зҡ„жһ¶иЁӯв”Җpure-ftpd pure-ftpdдҪңжҘӯеҜҰеҒҡзҙ°зҜҖ

entry.title: Konjunktiv II
entry.content: Konjunktiv IIWozu braucht man den Konjunktiv II?Mit dem Konjunktiv II kann man ausdrГјcken, dass etwas irreal ist. WГјnschen und TrГӨume Ich wГјrde gerne Urlaub machen.-> Ich mache keinen Urlaub. MГ¶glichkeiten und irreale Bedingungen Der Pilz kГ¶nnte giftig sein. Wenn ich Zeit hГӨtte, hГӨtte ich den Film geschaut. -> Ich habe keine Zeit. Warnungen und Vorschlagen An deiner Stelle wГјrde ich disen Pilz nicht eseen. HГ¶flichkeit Ich mГ¶chte gerne noch einen Kaffee bitte!Die Bildung in der Gegenwart1. wГјrde + Infinitiv Beispiel: Meine Eltern wГјrden mir ein neue Iphone nicht kaufen. ->Ich kaufe mir ein neue Iphone. 2.Verbformen des Konjunktiv II Die Bildung des Konjunktivs II mit den Verbformen Verben, die hГӨufig benutzt werden Die Modalverben Konjunktiv II in der VergangenheitAls Basis der Perfektform: вҖңhaben / sein + Partizip IIвҖқ Beispiele: Die Frau wГӨre nicht zu spГӨt gekommen. Der Mann hГӨtte ein neues Auto gekauft. Konjunktiv II der Vergangenheit mit Modalverben Beispiele: Harry hГӨtte gestern arbeiten mГјssen. Lisa hГӨtte nach Deutschland fliegen dГјrfen. Konjunktiv II und PassivKonjunktiv II der Gegenwart plus Passiv Beispiele: Der Artikel wГјrde bald korrigiert werden. Die TГјr wГјrde jetzt geschlossen werden. Konjunktiv II der Vergangenheit plus Passiv Beispiele: Das Fenster wГӨre gestern repariert worden. Der TГӨter wГӨre gefunden worden.

entry.title: Konjunktiv I und Indirekte Rede
entry.content: Indirekte RedeWofГјr braucht man die indirekte Rede?Die Indirekte Rede wurde benuzt, wenn Sie die Aussagen anderer ausdrГјcken mГ¶chten. Den Konjunktiv I benutzen wir im Deutschen fГјr die indirekte Rede und diese Form wurde hГӨufig in der Zeitung, in Nachrichten oder im Radio wervendet. Bildung der indirekten Rede Es gibt im Deutschen zwei wichtige Werkzeuge, mit denen man fremde Gedanken kenntlich machen kann: Der Gebrauch von so genannten Verben der Mitteilung (sagen, meinen, behaupten etc.) in Verbindung mit einem Nebensatz. Der Gebrauch des Konjunktiv I Beispiel: Der Mathelehrer sagt вҖң Die Mathematik ist gar nicht so einfach.вҖқ -> Der Matheklehrer sagt, dass die Mathematik gar nicht so einfach sei. Es funktioniert auch ohne Nebensatz: -> Der Mathelehrer sagt, die Mathematik sei gar nicht so einfach . Weitere Beispiel: Der Matheklehrer sagt, dass die Mathematik gar nicht so einfach sei. Man mГјsse die richtige Lerntechnik benutzen und viel mit dem Kopf denken. Die вҖҳmГјsseвҖҷ ist Kon. I. Es bedeutet der Satz ist immer noch die Meinung von dem MatheLeherer. Bildung des Konjunktiv IDie Regel fГјr schwache Verben lautet: Beispiele: FГјr spezialen Verben: Beispiel: Der Politiker sagte, er habe das nicht gewusst. Die WГјrde-FormIst die Verbform im Kon. I identisch mit dem Indikativ so verwendet man die Formen des Kon. II Beispiele: Gestern haben Paul und Erica gesagt, dass sie nГӨchste Woche nach Deutschland gehen. Sie lernen dort Deutsch. -> Gestern haben Paul und Erica gesagt, dass sie nГӨchste Woche nach Deutschland gehen. Sie wГјrden dort Deutsch lernen. Diese Partei wГӨhle ich nicht! Die Vorsitzenden lГјgen oft. Weil die Form Kon. I identisch mit dem Indikativ ist(lГјgen), kann man die als Kon. II verwenden oder auch die wГјrden Form benutzen. -> Paul sagt, dass er diese Parei nicht wГӨhle. Die Vorsitzenden lГ¶gen oft. oder: -> Paul sagt, dass er diese Parei nicht wГӨhle. Die Vorsitzenden wГјrden oft lГ¶gen. Diese Partei wГӨhle ich nicht! Die Vorsitzenden lernen nicht aus ihren Fehlen. -> Paul sagt, dass er diese Parei nicht wГӨhle. Die Vorsitzenden lernten nicht aus ihren Fehlen . Hier soll man nicht ВҙlerntenВҙ benutzen, denn das Indiktiv(PrГӨteritum) ist identisch wie Kon. I -> Paul sagt, dass er diese Parei nicht wГӨhle. Die Vorsitzenden wГјrden nicht aus ihren Fehlen lernen.

entry.title: Service and Setting
entry.content: Services & SettingsServices Commen Flow of Runing a Service Installation: Through ports, packages, or source tarballs pkg install kde4 Configuration Update /etc/rc.conf kdm4_enable="YESвҖқ service kdm4 enable Start rc.d/* /usr/local/etc/rc.d/kdm4 start service kdm4 start Maintenance Updating and Restarting Configuration File Local installed programsвҖҷ configuration files are located under /usr/local/etc Daemon вҶ’ program-name.conf pure-ftpd.conf user-program вҶ’ program-namerc vimrc screenrc Default config file usually installed with .sample or .default suffix copy and rename before use it A program with multiple config files are usually located in /usr/local/etc/program-name/ in a directory apache* RC Script Scripts for starting / stopping a service Command scripts for auto-reboot and daemon startup Start services on system startup Starting and Stopping services in a standard way Without rc: usr/local/sbin/pure-ftpd -g /var/run/pure-ftpd.pid -A -c50 -B -C8 -D -fftp -H -I15 -lpam -lunix -L10000:8 -m4 -s -U133:022 -u100 -k99 -Z With rc: service pure-ftpd start Launch daemon processes Launch Path to the executable binary/script /usr/sbin/inetd Configuration Program-specified configuration /etc/inetd.conf Pidfile Records (master) process id of the service /var/run/inetd.pid Use rc scipt ex:ntpd as rc.conf An easy way to access: вҖңserviceвҖқ $ service ntpd start/stop/restart/reload/вҖҰ Search /etc/rc.d and /usr/local/etc/rc.d Start start the service and write PID files now Stop killing the process with PID Restart Restart the service Enable/Disable Edit /etc/rc.conf with XXX_enable=вҖңYESвҖқ or вҖңNOвҖқ Reload Reload configuration file Status Check the service is running or not Local installed service More about how to use rc.conf for an installed service, read comments from that script /usr/local/etc/rc.d/pure-ftpd

entry.title: User Management
entry.content: #User Management Adding New UsersID User ID, Group ID Root Other Important Users daemon: owner of unprivileged software bin: owner of system commands sys: owner of the kernel and memory images nobody: owner of nothingSteps to add new user Edit the password and group files ->vipw, pw Set an initial password ->password harry Set quota ->edquota harry Create user home directory ->mkdir /home/harry Copy startup files to userвҖҷs home (optional) Set the file/directory owner to the user ->chown -R harry:dcs /home/harry password and group file /etc/passwd Store user information: Login name Encrypted password (* or x) UID Default GID GECOS information Full name, office, extension, home phone Home directory Login shell Encrypted password The encrypted password is stored in shadow file for security reason /etc/master.passwd (BSD) /etc/shadow (Linux) /etc/group Contains the names of UNIX groups and a list of each groupвҖҷs member: Group name Encrypted password GID Only in wheel group can do вҖңsuвҖқ command In FreeBSD Use вҖңvipwвҖқ to edit /etc/master.passwd In Linux Edit /etc/passwd and then Use вҖңpwconvвҖқ to transfer into /etc/shadow Remove accounts Delete the account entry [FreeBSD] vipw, pw userdel [Linux] remove the row in /etc/passwd and pwconv Backup file and mailbox 123tar jcf harry-home-20190927.tar.bz /home/harrytar jcf harry-mail-20190927.tar.bz /var/mail/harrychmod 600 liyu-*-20190927.tar.bz Delete home directory and mailbox 1rm -rf /home/harry /var/mail/harry Rootly Powers Root Root is God, A.K.A. super-user. UID is 0 UNIX permits super-user to perform any valid operation on any file or process, such as: Changing the root directory of a process with chroot Configuring network interfaces (ifconfig command) Shutting down the system (shutdown command) вҖҰ Login as root Remote login (login via ssh) sshd:/etc/ssh/sshd_config -> insecure #PermitRootLogin yes su : substitute user identity su, su -, su username sudo : a limited su (security/sudo) Subdivide superuserвҖҷs power Who can execute what command on which host as whom. Each command executed though sudo will be logged (/var/log/auth.log) -> trackable sudoers Who can execute what command on which host as whom modify /etc/sudoers to allow new authorities can use visudo to edit

entry.title: Git
entry.content: GitGit еҹәзӨҺжҰӮеҝөGit зүҲжң¬жҺ§еҲ¶ зӮәдәҶи§Јжұәзҡ„е•ҸйЎҢпјҡ зЁӢејҸдҝ®ж”№йҒҺеҫҢзҙҖйҢ„йә»з…© жҖ•зЁӢејҸж”№йҢҜеҸҲиҰҒйҮҚж”№пјҢеҸҲж”№дёҚеӣһдҫҶ еӨҡдәәеҗҲдҪңProjectжҷӮзЁӢејҸе®№жҳ“иў«иҰҶи“Ӣ git дҪҝз”ЁеҲҶж•ЈејҸзүҲжң¬жҺ§еҲ¶зі»зөұ иө·еҲқжҳҜ Linus torvaldsеӨ§её«зӮәдәҶиҰҒжҺ§еҲ¶linux kernelзүҲжң¬иҖҢеҺ»й–Ӣзҷјзҡ„е·Ҙе…· git е„Әй»һ жӘ”жЎҲзҡ„зӢҖж…ӢпјҲstatusпјүдҪңзӮәжӣҙж–°жӯ·еҸІиЁҳйҢ„дҝқеӯҳ еҸҜд»Ҙеҫ©еҺҹеҲ°д»ҘеүҚзҡ„жӘ”жЎҲзӢҖж…Ӣ еҸҜд»ҘйЎҜзӨәз·ЁијҜйҒҺеҫҢе…§е®№зҡ„е·®з•°з”Ё Repository дҫҶз®ЎзҗҶжӯ·еҸІзҙҖйҢ„ RepositoryеҠҹиғҪ иЁҳйҢ„жӘ”жЎҲжҲ–зӣ®йҢ„зӢҖж…Ӣ е„Іеӯҳе…§е®№зҡ„жӯ·еҸІдҝ®ж”№иЁҳйҢ„ иҝҪи№Өе…§е®№зҡ„зӢҖж…Ӣе’ҢзүҲжң¬ Local Repository and Remote Repository Local Repository: зөҰеӨ§е®¶дёҖиө·еҲҶдә«зҡ„ й…ҚжңүдјәжңҚеҷЁ Remote Repository: ж–№дҫҝз”ЁеҖӢдәәз”Ё Create Repo еңЁlocalз«Ҝе»әз«ӢдёҰinit Repo pull fromйҒ з«Ҝзҡ„ RepoGit Commit з”Ё git commit еҸҜд»ҘжҠҠж–°еўһзҡ„жқұиҘҝдёҹеҲ°Repoдёӯ еҹ·иЎҢе®Ң commitеҫҢ Repoжңғз”ўз”ҹдёҖеҖӢRevisionдҫҶзҙҖйҢ„ж–°зүҲиҲҠзүҲй–“зҡ„е·®з•° Tip:дёҚеҗҢеҗ«ж„Ҹзҡ„жӣҙж”№зӣЎйҮҸеҸҜд»ҘеҲҶй–ӢдҫҶжҸҗдәӨд»Ҙдҫҝж—ҘеҫҢжҹҘзңӢпјҲex:debug, new functionпјү Tip: CommitжҷӮжҸҗдәӨиЁҠжҒҜжҮүеҢ…еҗ« дҝ®ж”№е…§е®№зҡ„ж‘ҳиҰҒ дҝ®ж”№зҡ„зҗҶз”ұ Working Tree and IndexпјҲStaging Areaпјү Working Tree иІ иІ¬дҝқеӯҳзӣ®еүҚжӯЈеңЁиҷ•зҗҶжӘ”жЎҲзҡ„зӣ®йҢ„йӮ„жңүgit жҢҮд»Өзҡ„зӣёй—ңж“ҚдҪң IndexпјҲStaging AreaпјүжҳҜз•¶еңЁдәӨеҲ°remote Repoй–“зҡ„дёҖеҖӢжӘ”жЎҲжҡ«еӯҳеҚҖ й–ӢдҪҝз”Ё GitPrerequisite Create a Github Account Git Install gitk еҸҜз”ЁGUIдҫҶзўәиӘҚжҸҗдәӨжӯ·еҸІ 1Sudo apt install git Git з’°еўғиЁӯе®ҡ123456git config --global user.name "Github-user-name"git config --global user.email "Github-uesd-email"git config --global color.ui truegit config --global core.editor vimgit config --global alias.co commitgit config --global alias.lg "log --color --graph --all --pretty=format:'%Cred%h%Creset -%C(yellow)%d%Creset %s %Cgreen(%cr) %C(bold blue)<%an>%Creset' --abbrev-commit --" Create Repo123mkdir testcd testgit init е»әдёҖеҖӢиіҮж–ҷеӨҫдёҰйҖІеҺ»иЈЎйқўеҒҡ initialization Commit Files з”ЁдҫҶзңӢWork Tree е’Ң Index зӢҖж…Ӣ 1git status е°ҮжӘ”жЎҲеҠ е…ҘIndex пјҲStaging Areaпјү 1234git add <file>##Add all filesgit add. иӢҘжӘ”жЎҲжңүжӣҙж”№ жҲ–йңҖиҰҒжҜ”ијғжӘ”жЎҲе·®еҲҘ 12#еҸҜз”ЁжҜ”ијғе…©ж¬ЎдёҠеӮіжӘ”жЎҲд№Ӣй–“зҡ„е·®еҲҘ git diff дёҠеӮіжӘ”жЎҲеҲ°remote Repo 12# -mд»ЈиЎЁиҰҒдёҹдёҠеҺ»зөҰеҲҘдәәзңӢзҡ„иЁҠжҒҜ commit messagegit commit -m "origin master" зңӢжӯ·еҸІжҸҗдәӨзҙҖйҢ„ 1git log Shared Ropo Push :еҲ°йҒ з«Ҝзҡ„Repo Clone:еҫһйҒ з«Ҝзҡ„RepoиӨҮиЈҪжӘ”жЎҲ Pull :еҗҢжӯҘйҒ з«Ҝзҡ„Repo дҫҶжӣҙж–°жң¬ең°зҡ„Repo йҖЈзөҗеҲ°йҒ з«Ҝ Repo еңЁGithubеүөдёҖеҖӢж–°зҡ„ Repo дёҰдё”еӢҫйҒёдёҚз”Ё.gitignore README.md LICENSEеҰӮдёӢ .gitignore жҳҜз”ЁдҫҶз®ЎзҗҶдёҚжғіиў«дёҹдёҠеҺ»зҡ„жқұиҘҝ,README.md еүҮжҳҜеҒҡзҙҖйҢ„зҡ„жӘ”жЎҲ еҺ»иӨҮиЈҪиҮӘе·ұзҡ„ еңЁGithub дёҠзҡ„ Repositoryзҡ„ ssh з¶ІеқҖеҰӮдёӢ`git@github.com:username/git_test.git` з”Ёд»ҘдёӢжҢҮд»Өе°Үжң¬ең°з«Ҝзҡ„иіҮж–ҷеӨҫе’ҢйҒ з«ҜRepoйҖЈзөҗ 1git remote add origin git@github.com:username/git_test.git дёҰдҪҝз”Ёд»ҘдёӢжҢҮд»ӨдҫҶе°ҮжӘ”жЎҲpush дёҠеҺ»йҒ з«Ҝ Repo 1git push -u origin master йҖҡеёёзі»зөұжңғиҰҒжұӮијёе…ҘдҪ зҡ„йҒ з«Ҝеёіиӯ·еҗҚзЁұе’ҢеҜҶзўј,еҸҜз”Ёд»Ҙssh-keygenдҫҶзңҒдёӢжҜҸж¬ЎйғҪиҰҒијёе…ҘеҜҶзўјзҡ„йә»з…© 12345678910111213#з”ўз”ҹдҪ йӣ»и…Ұзҡ„е…¬й‘°з§Ғй‘°ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "your_email@gmail.com"#жүҖз”ҹжҲҗзҡ„е…¬й‘°е’Ңз§Ғй‘°еңЁ ~/.ssh/зӣ®йҢ„дёӢ id_rsa е’Ңid_rsa.pubдёӯ#иӨҮиЈҪе…¬й‘°дёӯзҡ„е…§е®№cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub ``` йҡЁеҫҢеҲ°GitHubз¶Ій Ғзҡ„SettingдёҠж–°еўһдҪ зҡ„Public SSH key ![](https://i.imgur.com/rx0jB0G.png)- з”Ёgit cloneжҢҮд»ӨдҫҶиӨҮиЈҪйҒ з«Ҝзҡ„Repo git clone #ex: git clone https://github.com/h123a456rr789y/HackMD_Note.git HackMD_Note1- з”Ёgit pullжҢҮд»ӨдҫҶеҗҢжӯҘйҒ з«Ҝзҡ„Repo git pull git pull origin master123456789101112## иҷ•зҗҶеҗҲдҪөдҝ®ж”№зҙҖйҢ„- еҰӮжһңдҪ еңЁpushеүҚпјҢжңүе…¶д»–дәәpush жӣҙж–°дәҶйҒ з«Ҝзҡ„RepoиҖҢжІ’жңүжӣҙж–°еҲ°дҪ зҡ„жң¬ең°з«ҜпјҢдҪ зҡ„pushе°ұжңғеӨұж•—пјҢжӯӨжҷӮе°ұйңҖиҰҒеҗҲдҪөеңЁpushдёҠеҺ»- и§Јжұәж–№жі•: - е…ҲpullеңЁpush:з”Ёrebaseзҡ„ж–№ејҸеҗҲдҪө(дёҚжҳҜиҗ¬иғҪ) еҰӮжһңдёҚиЎҢеүҮйңҖжүӢеӢ•еҗҲдҪө `git pull --rebase` - дёҚз®Ўд»–зҡ„еј·еҲ¶pushжҲ‘зҸҫеңЁзҡ„дёҠеҺ»: -f жҳҜеј·еҲ¶зҡ„ж„ҸжҖқ (**еҲҘдәӮз”Ё**) `git push -f`### Rebase еҗҲдҪөиЈңе……1. жңғе…ҲжҠҠжң¬ең° repo. еҫһдёҠж¬Ў pull д№ӢеҫҢзҡ„и®Ҡжӣҙжҡ«еӯҳиө·дҫҶ 2. еӣһеҫ©еҲ°дёҠж¬Ў pull жҷӮзҡ„жғ…жіҒ 3. еҘ—з”ЁйҒ з«Ҝзҡ„и®Ҡжӣҙ 4. жңҖеҫҢеҶҚеҘ—з”Ёеүӣжҡ«еӯҳдёӢдҫҶзҡ„жң¬ең°и®Ҡжӣҙ #еҗҲдҪөеүҚ DвҖ”EвҖ“F master /AвҖ”BвҖ”CвҖ”G origin/master #Rebase еҗҲдҪө AвҖ”BвҖ”CвҖ”GвҖ”DвҖҷвҖ”EвҖҷвҖ”FвҖҷ master, origin/master #merge еҗҲдҪө D---E----F / \ AвҖ”BвҖ”CвҖ”GвҖ”-H master, origin/master` жүҖд»ҘдёҖж—ҰеҮәзҸҫconflict е°ұз„Ўжі•еҹ·иЎҢrebaseпјҢйңҖиҰҒжүӢеӢ•дҝ®еҫ©еҫҢпјҢз„¶еҫҢжүҚеҸҜд»Ҙз№јзәҢеӢ•дҪң merge еҰӮжһңзҷјз”ҹ conflictпјҢдҪ еҸӘйңҖиҰҒи§ЈжұәиЎқзӘҒдёҖж¬ЎпјҢеҗҲжҲҗдёҖеҖӢж–°пјҢз„¶еҫҢе°ұеҸҜ commit еҮәеҺ» ж”№еҫ—жҜ”ијғеӨҡ -> жңғжңүијғеӨҡзҡ„ conflict -> mergeж”№еҫ—жҜ”ијғе°‘ -> жңғжңүијғе°‘зҡ„ conflict -> rebase еҲҶж”ҜBranch е°Үдҝ®ж”№иЁҳйҢ„зҡ„ж•ҙй«”жөҒзЁӢеҲҶй–Ӣе„ІеӯҳпјҢи®“еҲҶй–Ӣзҡ„еҲҶж”ҜдёҚеҸ—е…¶д»–еҲҶж”Ҝзҡ„еҪұйҹҝпјҢжүҖд»ҘеңЁеҗҢдёҖеҖӢж•ёж“ҡеә«иЈЎеҸҜд»ҘеҗҢжҷӮйҖІиЎҢеӨҡеҖӢдёҚеҗҢзҡ„дҝ®ж”№ еҲҶж”Ҝй–“еҸҜд»ҘеҗҲдҪө еҲҶж”Ҝзҡ„йҒӢз”Ё IntegrationеҲҶж”Ҝ(дё»иҰҒзҡ„еҲҶж”Ҝ):дҝқжҢҒз©©е®ҡжҳҜеҫҲйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„пјҢеӣ зӮәж–°зҡ„еҲҶж”Ҝжңғе»әз«ӢеңЁе®ғзҡ„дёҠйқўпјҢиҖҢдё”IntegrationеҲҶж”ҜжҳҜзӮәдәҶеҸҜд»ҘйҡЁжҷӮе»әз«ӢзҷјеёғзүҲжң¬зҡ„еҲҶж”Ҝ TopicеҲҶж”Ҝ(ж¬ЎиҰҒеҲҶж”Ҝ):TopicеҲҶж”ҜжҳҜзӮәдәҶй–ӢзҷјеҠҹиғҪжҲ–дҝ®еҫ©йҢҜиӘӨд№ӢйЎһзҡ„д»»еӢҷжүҖе»әз«Ӣзҡ„еҲҶж”ҜгҖӮиӢҘеҗҢжҷӮйҖІиЎҢеӨҡеҖӢд»»еӢҷжҷӮпјҢжӮЁеҝ…й Ҳе»әз«ӢеӨҡеҖӢзҡ„TopicеҲҶж”ҜпјҢдҝ®ж”№е®ҢеҫҢеҶҚе°ҮTopicеҲҶж”ҜеҗҲдҪөеҲ°IntergrationеҲҶж”ҜеҲҶж”Ҝзҡ„еҲҮжҸӣ з”Ё checkoutжҢҮд»Ө:е·ҘдҪңзӣ®йҢ„иЈЎзҡ„жӘ”жЎҲжңғж №ж“ҡеҲҮжҸӣеҲ°дёҚеҗҢзҡ„еҲҶж”ҜиҖҢе‘ҲзҸҫи©ІеҲҶж”ҜжңҖеҫҢжҸҗдәӨзҡ„е…§е®№ HEADд»ЈиЎЁз•¶еүҚеҲҶж”Ҝзҡ„жңҖж–°жҸҗдәӨеҗҚзЁұ

entry.title: Isi und Ossi
entry.content: вҖҡIsi und OssiвҖҳ ist eine deutsche LiebeskomГ¶die aus dem Jahr 2020. Es handelt sich um die erste deutsche Netflix-Film-Eigenproduktion. Die Hauptrollen verkГ¶pern Lisa Vicari und Dennis Mojen und der Film wurde von Oliver Kienle produziet. Isi kommt aus einer sehr reichen Familie in Heidelberg, die sich gar nicht fГјr die Zukunft sorgen muss. Jedoch mГ¶chte sie eine Kochausbildung in New York machen und eine KГ¶chin werden, sodass ihre Eltern sich in den Weg stellen und sie ein BWL Studium erwarten. Im Gegensazt kommt Ossi aus einer pleiten Familie in Mannheim. Seine Mutter besiztet eine Tankstelle und hat viele Schulden. Sein GroГҹvater ist ein verwahrloster Krimineller, der neulich aus einem GefГӨngis entlassen wurde. Aufgrund der Geldprobleme konnte er seinen Traum als professioneller Boxer nicht ermГ¶glichen. Als die beiden sich zufГӨllig begegnen, verfolgt Isi den Plan, dass Ossi sich vor ihren Eltern als asozialer Freund benehmen soll, um diese zu erpressen. DafГјr finanziert Isi ihm seinen Kampf, der Eintritt in die Profiliga, und rettet die Tankstelle seiner Mutter vor dem Bankrott. Was als Spiel beginnt, wird jedoch plГ¶ztlich ernst, sie haben sich darГјber hinaus tatsГӨchlich ineinander verliebt und sind durcheinander. Was wichtiger ist: Liebe oder Karriere? Letztendlich, hat Isi nach einigen Verwicklungen auf die Kochausbildung verzichtet und stattdessen hat sie einen Burgerladen aufgekauft. Ossi hat schlieГҹlich den Kampf gewonnen und die Geschichte zwischen dem sГјГҹen Paar entfaltet sich auf eine ungewisse Zukunft. Die Szene, in welcher Isi auffortdert вҖһKannst du mich bitte richtig asozial kГјssenвҖң, ist total sГјГҹ. Obwohl die Karriere, die FamiliehintergrГјnde und die PersonalitГӨt so ungleich sind, die beiden haben sich einfach so verliebt. Es geht darum, dass die Hauptsache der Liebe immer das authentische GefГјhl ist, wenn du extrem reich bist oder dein Partner bzw. Partnerin extrem reicht ist.

entry.title: еӯёзҝ’дёҖеҖӢз¬¬дәҢеӨ–иӘһ --- еҫ·ж–Ү
entry.content: дёҖеҲҮзҡ„й–Ӣе§Ӣ вҖ” е•ҹи’ҷ2013е№ҙжҷӮжҲ‘иЁҲз•«йҖҸйҒҺжү¶ијӘзӨҫзҡ„дәӨжҸӣеҲ°еҫ·еңӢдәӨжҸӣдёҖе№ҙпјҢеңЁйҖҷи¶ҹйҒ еҫҒд№ӢеүҚпјҢе°Қеҫ·ж–Үе®Ңе…ЁжІ’жҺҘи§ёйҒҺзҡ„жҲ‘пјҢжұәе®ҡзӮәдәҶйҖҷеҖӢдәӨжҸӣеҲ°й«ҳйӣ„ж–Үи—»еӨ–иӘһеӯёйҷўй–Ӣзҡ„еҫ·ж–ҮиӘІеҺ»дёҠдёҖжңҹA1зҡ„еҹәзӨҺиӘІгҖӮзӮәжңҹ10е ӮиӘІдёҖж¬Ў2е°ҸжҷӮзҡ„еҹәзӨҺеҫ·ж–ҮиӘІпјҢжҳҜжҲ‘й–Ӣе§ӢжҺҘи§ёеҫ·еңӢж–ҮеҢ–гҖҒеӯёзҝ’еӯ—жҜҚгҖҒзҷјйҹізҡ„й–Ӣе§ӢпјҢйҖҸйҒҺе°Қж–јйҖҷдәӣж–ҮеҢ–зҡ„дәҶи§ЈиҗҢзҷјжҲ‘е°Қеҫ·еңӢиҲҮеҫ·иӘһзҡ„иҲҲи¶ЈпјҢд№ҹеӯёзҝ’еҲ°дәҶдёҖдәӣз°Ўе–®зҡ„и©һеҪҷеҸҠеҸҘеӯҗпјҢдёҖжңҹзҡ„иӘІеҫҲеҝ«зөҗжқҹдәҶпјҢжә–еӮҷиёҸдёҠеҫ·еңӢдәӨжҸӣзҡ„ж—…зЁӢпјҢжҳҜи©ІдҫҶй©—иӯүиҮӘе·ұеӯёзҝ’жҲҗжһңзҡ„жҷӮеҖҷдәҶгҖӮ дәӨжҸӣзҡ„й–Ӣе§Ӣ вҖ” и©Ұз…үеңЁз¶“йҒҺ18еҖӢе°ҸжҷӮжј«й•·зҡ„ж—…зЁӢпјҢжҲ‘зөӮж–јжҠөйҒ”дәҶеҫ·еңӢзҡ„еҜ„е®ҝе®¶еәӯеңЁеҫ·еңӢиҘҝеҢ—йғЁ(Esens)ж—Ғзҡ„дёҖеҖӢжқ‘еӯҗStedesdorfпјҢиө·еҲқжҲ‘еғҸжүҖжңүдәӨжҸӣз”ҹжңҹеҫ…и‘—жҺҘеҫ…е®¶еәӯеҫҲжңүиҖҗеҝғзҡ„з”ЁиӢұж–Үи·ҹжҲ‘жәқйҖҡпјҢж…ўж…ўзҡ„еӯёзҝ’дёҰзҶҹжӮүйҖҷеҖӢж–ҮеҢ–еҸҠиӘһиЁҖпјҢдҪҶжҳҜжҲ‘зҡ„иҪҹе®¶дёҰжІ’жңүзөҰжҲ‘еӨӘеӨҡе–ҳжҒҜзҡ„з©әй–“,дёҰе‘ҠиЁҙжҲ‘пјҢд»–еҖ‘еңЁе…©е‘Ёд№ӢеҫҢйҷӨдәҶйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„дәӢжғ…еӨ–еҸӘжңғиҲҮжҲ‘з”Ёеҫ·иӘһе°Қи©ұпјҢйҖҷжҳҜдёҖйЎҶеӮ‘е…Ӣзҡ„иұҢиұҶпјҢзЁ®еңЁжҲ‘зҡ„еҝғеә•дёҰдё”иҝ«дҪҝи‘—жҲ‘еҚіе°ҮиҰҒжңүзӘҒз ҙеӨ©йҡӣзҡ„жҲҗй•·пјҢз„¶иҖҢжҲ‘зҡ„иҪҹе®¶еңЁе°Қж–јжҲ‘жңҹиЁұзҡ„еҗҢжҷӮпјҢд№ҹзөҰдәҲжҲ‘еҫҲеӨҡзҡ„е№«еҠ©д»ҘеҸҠдёҖдәӣе°Ҹж–№жі•пјҢдёҰдё”еҫҲйЎҳж„ҸеңЁзҷҫеҝҷдёӯиҠұжҷӮй–“и·ҹжҲ‘жәқйҖҡгҖҒиҒҠеӨ©гҖҒи¬ӣи§ЈгҖҒж•ҷеӯёгҖӮ иЁҳеҫ—еңЁжҲ‘дёҖжҠөйҒ”иҪҹе®¶зҡ„жҷӮеҖҷпјҢйӨҗе»ігҖҒжҲҝй–“гҖҒе®ўе»іиІји‘—еҗ„еҖӢжқұиҘҝе–®еӯ—зҡ„е°ҸзҙҷжўқпјҢд»–еҖ‘д№ҹеңЁеё¶жҲ‘зҶҹжӮүе®¶иЈЎз’°еўғжҷӮиӘҚиӯҳдәҶйҖҷдәӣе–®еӯ—пјҢжҜҸж¬ЎзңӢеҲ°е°ұз·ҙзҝ’дёҖж¬ЎпјҢдёҖе‘ЁеҫҢжҲ‘е°ұе°ҮйҖҷдәӣе–®еӯ—е…ЁйғЁиЁҳдәҶиө·дҫҶгҖӮжӯӨеӨ–пјҢжҲ‘зҡ„иҪҹе®¶д№ҹеҫҲз”Ёеҝғзҡ„зӮәжҲ‘жә–еӮҷе…’з«Ҙж•…дәӢжӣёеҸҠе…’з«ҘCDпјҢдёҰдё”еӣ‘е’җжҲ‘еңЁеӯёзҝ’жҷӮеҸҜд»Ҙе°Үж–Үеӯ—еӨ§иҒІе”ёеҮәдҫҶпјҢеңЁзқЎеүҚиҒҶиҒҪCDдёҰиҒҶиҒҪе…ҘзқЎпјҢйҖҷжЁЈеҸҜд»ҘжңүеҠ©ж–јеңЁзқЎзң жҷӮжҢҒзәҢеӨ§и…Ұзҡ„жҪӣеңЁеӯёзҝ’пјҢеҗҢжҷӮд№ҹеҸҜд»ҘеўһеҠ е°Қж–јиӘһиЁҖзҡ„ж„ҹиҰәгҖӮйҷӨжӯӨд№ӢеӨ–пјҢжҲ‘дҪҝз”ЁдәҶ Rosetta Stone зҡ„з·ҡдёҠеӯёзҝ’иӘһиЁҖз¶Із«ҷпјҢдё”еңЁжҜҸеҖӢжҷҡдёҠз·ҙзҝ’зҙ„дёҖеҖӢе°ҸжҷӮпјҢеҗҢжҷӮжҲ‘д№ҹдҪҝз”ЁLEOз·ҡдёҠеӯ—е…ёдҫҶжҹҘжҲ‘дёҚжңғзҡ„е–®еӯ—пјҢдёҰдё”дҪҝз”ЁдёҖй ҒеҸҜд»ҘеҜ«зҙ„5еҖӢе–®еӯ—е°ҸзӯҶиЁҳжң¬дҫҶиЁҳйҢ„жҲ‘еӯёзҝ’еҲ°зҡ„ж–°е–®еӯ—еҖ‘гҖӮ еңЁеӯёж ЎпјҢжҲ‘з•¶жҷӮеңЁеҫ·еңӢијғеҘҪзҡ„й«ҳдёӯ(Gymnasium)зҡ„еҚҒе№ҙзҙҡе°ұи®ҖпјҢжҲ‘зҡ„иҪҹе®¶жңғиҰҒжұӮжҲ‘з©ҚжҘөеҸғиҲҮеӯёж Ўзҡ„иӘІзЁӢиҲҮиҖғи©ҰпјҢиЁҳеҫ—з¬¬дёҖеӨ©ж”ҫеӯёеӣһе®¶пјҢиҪҹеӘҪе•ҸжҲ‘:еӯёж ЎжңүжІ’жңүжҙҫд»»дҪ•дҪңжҘӯпјҢжҲ‘еӣһйҒ“:жңүдёҖзҜҮз”ҹзү©зҡ„ж–Үз« иҰҒи®ҖпјҢжҲ‘иЁҳеҫ—йӮЈжҳҜдёҖеҖӢжңүй—ңжј”еҢ–и«–зҡ„ж–Үз« пјҢжҲ‘жүҚеҲ°еҫ·еңӢдёҖе‘ЁпјҢйҖЈдёҖдәӣеҹәжң¬зҡ„е–®еӯ—йғҪйӮ„дёҚжңғпјҢдҪҶжҲ‘йӮ„жҳҜзЎ¬и‘—й ӯзҡ®жҠҠжүҖжңүе–®еӯ—жҹҘе®ҢдәҶпјҢдёҚз¶“ж„ҹеҳҶ:гҖҢдәәз”ҹзңҹйӣЈпјҢдҪҶеҫ·ж–ҮжӣҙйӣЈйҳҝ!гҖҚпјҢжӯӨеӨ–пјҢеӯёж Ўзҡ„иҖғи©Ұд№ҹд»ӨжҲ‘й ӯз–јпјҢжҲ‘еҸӘиғҪи©Ұи‘—е°ҮжҲ‘жңғзҡ„жӢјж№ҠжҲҗеҸҘеӯҗеҜ«жҲҗж–Үз« пјҢж•ёеӯёзү©зҗҶеҢ–еӯёйӮ„зЁҚеҫ®жңүй»һй ӯз·’пјҢиҖҢе…¶д»–йңҖиҰҒж–Үеӯ—дҫҶиЎЁйҒ”жғіжі•зҡ„з§‘зӣ®д»ӨжҲ‘жҢ«жҠҳпјҢдҪҶе№ёйҒӢзҡ„жҳҜпјҢжҲ‘зҡ„иҪҹе®¶еҫҲйЎҳж„ҸиҠұжҷӮй–“ж•ҷжҲ‘пјҢе‘ҠиЁҙжҲ‘еҫ·еңӢдәәеҰӮдҪ•еҜ«дёҖзҜҮж–Үз« зҡ„жҖқз¶ӯд»ҘеҸҠжғіжі•гҖӮ жҲ‘д№ҹи©Ұи‘—еңЁеӯёж ЎиҲҮеҗҢеӯёз”Ёеҫ·ж–ҮиӘӘи©ұпјҢдёҰдё”и«Ӣе®ғеҖ‘ж•ҷжҲ‘дёҖдәӣжқұиҘҝзҡ„еҫ·ж–ҮпјҢиӘӘдҫҶеҘҮжҖӘпјҢз¶“йҒҺдәҶйҖҷйәјеӨҡе№ҙпјҢжҲ‘з”ҡиҮійӮ„иЁҳеҫ—жҹҗдәӣе–®еӯ—жҳҜеңЁд»Җйәје ҙжҷҜдёӢеӯёзҝ’зҡ„пјҢиЁҳеҫ—дёҖе ӮзҫҺиЎ“иӘІпјҢжҲ‘жҠҠжүҖжңүйүӣзӯҶзӣ’иЈЎйқўзҡ„жқұиҘҝйғҪе•Ҹе®ҢдәҶпјҢжңүдәӣз”ҡиҮійҖҷдәӣе№ҙдҫҶеҫһжІ’еҶҚиӘӘйҒҺзҡ„е–®еӯ—пјҢд№ҹеңЁжҲ‘и…Ұжө·иЈЎиЁҳеҫ—йқһеёёжё…жҘҡгҖӮ еңЁиҪҹе®¶дёӯпјҢжҲ‘зҡ„е®¶еәӯиҰҒжұӮжҲ‘жҜҸеӨ©жҷҡдёҠе…«й»һжә–жҷӮеңЁе®ўе»ійӣҶеҗҲпјҢе’Ңд»–еҖ‘дёҖиө·зңӢеҫ·еңӢз¬¬дёҖйӣ»иҰ–еҸ°зҡ„15еҲҶйҗҳжң¬ж—ҘйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„ж–°иҒһпјҢдёҰжңүжҷӮйӮҖи«ӢжҲ‘жҷҡдёҠе’Ңе®ғеҖ‘дёҖиө·зңӢеҫ·еңӢйӣ»еҪұпјҢдҪҶжҳҜз•¶дёҖеҖӢдәәз–ІжҶҠзҡ„жҷӮеҖҷпјҢиҖіиЈЎиҒҪеҲ°еҸҲжҳҜиҒҪдёҚжҮӮзҡ„иҒІйҹіжҷӮпјҢеёёеёёе°ұжңғдёҚе°ҸеҝғзқЎи‘—пјҢеҸҜжҳҜжІ’дәӢпјҢеҚідҫҝеңЁзқЎиҰәзҡ„жҷӮеҖҷпјҢйӮ„жҳҜеңЁеӯёеҫ·ж–Үе‘ў! дёҖеҖӢдјјжҜҚиӘһзҡ„еӨ–иӘһ вҖ” жҳҮиҸҜеңЁз¶“йҒҺдёҖеҖӢеӨҡжңҲзҡ„жҙ—зҰ®еҫҢпјҢжҲ‘зҡ„з”ҹжҙ»е№ҫд№ҺеҸӘеү©дёӢеҫ·ж–ҮпјҢиӢұж–Үзҡ„и§ЈйҮӢеҶҚд№ҹдёҚйңҖиҰҒдәҶпјҢз„¶иҖҢиӘһиЁҖеӯёзҝ’д№Ӣи·ҜдёҰйқһеҰӮжӯӨзҡ„з°Ўе–®пјҢиҰҒеҰӮдҪ•и¬ӣи©ұжүҚиғҪеғҸдёҖеҖӢйҒ“ең°зҡ„еҫ·еңӢдәәе‘ў? жҲ‘еҫҲж…¶е№ёзҡ„пјҢжҲ‘еңЁеӯёзҝ’иӘһиЁҖзҡ„йҒҺзЁӢдёӯжІ’жңүеҸ—еҲ°еӨӘеӨҡеҲ¶ејҸиӘһиЁҖиӘІзҡ„жқҹзёӣпјҢиҖҢжҳҜжңүиҮӘе·ұеҺ»жҺўзҙўдәҶи§ЈиӘһиЁҖзҡ„з©әй–“пјҢжҲ‘еҫһиҒҪ->иӘӘ->и®Җ->еҜ«пјҢдёҖжӯҘдёҖеӯёиө·пјҢе°ұеҘҪеғҸеңЁеӯёжҜҚиӘһдёҖжЁЈпјҢз”ЁиҖіжңөиҒҪпјҢз”Ёеҳҙе·ҙиӘӘпјҢз”Ёи…ҰиўӢзҢңпјҢжңҖйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„з„Ўйқһе°ұжҳҜжғіиЎЁйҒ”иҮӘе·ұзҡ„жғіжі•пјҢдёҰдё”еңЁз’°еўғзҡ„й©…дҪҝдёӢпјҢж…ўж…ўзҙҜз©ҚиҮӘе·ұзҡ„иӘһиЁҖиғҪеҠӣгҖӮ еңЁеӯёзҝ’иӘһиЁҖеҫҲйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„дёҖй»һжЁЎд»ҝд»ҘеҸҠеӢҮж•ўең°иӘӘпјҢиӘһиЁҖеҫҲйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„дёҖй»һе°ұжҳҜжәқйҖҡиҲҮиЎЁйҒ”пјҢеҚғиҗ¬дёҚиҰҒе®іжҖ•иҮӘе·ұжңғиӘӘйҢҜпјҢе°ұйҖЈеҫҲеӨҡеҫ·еңӢдәәиҮӘе·ұйғҪжңғеңЁи¬ӣи©ұзҡ„жҷӮеҖҷз”ЁйҢҜж–Үжі•пјҢжӣҙдҪ•жіҒеӨ–еңӢдәәпјҢеңЁиҒҠеӨ©жҷӮпјҢеӯёд»–еҖ‘зҡ„еҸЈиӘһз”Ёи©һпјҢе®ғеҖ‘д№ҹжңғиҰәеҫ—еҫҲжңүиҰӘеҲҮж„ҹпјҢеҰӮжһңзңҹзҡ„жІ’жңүеӢҮж°ЈпјҢйӮЈе°ұе–қе–қй…’еЈҜиҶҪеҗ§!еҫҲжңүж•Ҳ! ж…ўж…ўзҡ„дҪ жңғи¶ҠдҫҶи¶ҠжңғиӘӘпјҢи¶ҠдҫҶи¶ҠеғҸеңЁең°дәәзҡ„еҸЈйҹігҖӮ жӯӨеӨ–пјҢиҒҜжғізҡ„еӯёзҝ’ж–№ејҸд№ҹжҳҜеҫҲйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„пјҢеҰӮжһңдҪ д»ҠеӨ©дёҚзҹҘйҒ“дёҖеҖӢжқұиҘҝжҖҺйәјиӘӘпјҢдҪ иҰҒи©Ұи‘—з”ЁдҪ жңғзҡ„ж–Үеӯ—дҫҶиЎЁйҒ”дҪ жүҖиҰҒиӘӘзҡ„жқұиҘҝпјҢжҜ”еҰӮиӘӘпјҢдҪ дёҚжңғиӘӘзў—иЈЎзҡ„иҳӢжһңпјҢеҰӮжһңдҪ еҸӘзҹҘйҒ“иҳӢжһңиҖҢдёҚзҹҘйҒ“зў—зҡ„е–®еӯ—зҡ„и©ұпјҢи©Ұи‘—з”ЁиҳӢжһңдҫҶжҸҸиҝ°дё”еј•е°ҺеҲҘдәәе‘ҠиЁҙдҪ пјҢзў—иҰҒжҖҺйәјиӘӘпјҢжӯӨжҷӮпјҢдҪ дёҚдҪҶеӯёзҝ’еҲ°дәҶзў—йҖҷеҖӢж–°зҡ„и©һеҪҷпјҢйӮ„жңүиӨҮзҝ’дәҶиҳӢжһңпјҢжӣҙйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„жҳҜдҪ еӯёжңғдәҶиҰҒжҖҺйәјиЎЁйҒ”дёҰйҖЈжҺҘдәҶдҪ жғіиЎЁйҒ”зҡ„жқұиҘҝеҖ‘пјҢз•¶з„¶еңЁеӯёзҝ’жҠҪиұЎзҡ„дәӢжғ…дёҠйқўпјҢйҖҷд№ҹжҳҜеҫҲйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„пјҢжҳҜдёҖеҖӢеҗҢжҷӮеҸҜд»Ҙеўһеј·дҪ зҡ„иЁҳжҶ¶дё”еӯёзҝ’ж–°зҡ„жқұиҘҝзҡ„пјҢдёҖиҲүе…©еҫ—зҡ„еҘҪж–№жі•пјҢдёҰдё”еҸҜд»ҘеҠ еј·дҪ з”ЁеӨ–иӘһжҖқиҖғзҡ„иғҪеҠӣгҖӮ йӮ„жңүдёҖй»һжҳҜе°ҚиӘһиЁҖзҡ„ж„ҹиҰәпјҢдёҠйқўжҲ‘еҲҶдә«дәҶеҫҲеӨҡжҲ‘еӯёзҝ’иӘһиЁҖеҹ№йӨҠиӘһж„ҹзҡ„ж–№ејҸпјҢеғҸз”Ёе°Ҹе–®еӯ—еҚЎгҖҒзңӢз«ҘжӣёгҖҒиҒҪз«Ҙи©ұж•…дәӢгҖҒеӨҡиӘӘи©ұгҖҒеӨҡжЁЎд»ҝпјҢдёҚеӨ–д№Һе°ұжҳҜеўһеҠ дҪ е°ҚйҖҷеҖӢиӘһиЁҖзҡ„жҺҘи§ёиҲҮеҹ№йӨҠж„ҹжғ…пјҢжӯӨеӨ–пјҢиӘһиЁҖиҲҮж–ҮеҢ–жҳҜзӣёиј”зӣёжҲҗзҡ„пјҢеңЁеӯёзҝ’иӘһиЁҖзҡ„еҗҢжҷӮпјҢиӢҘиғҪеӨ жҗӯй…Қи‘—ж–ҮеҢ–дёҖиө·дәҶи§ЈпјҢе°ҮеҸҜд»ҘеҠ ж·ұдҪ зҡ„еҚ°иұЎгҖӮиҰҒеӯёзҝ’еҘҪдёҖеҖӢиӘһиЁҖзҡ„дёҚдәҢжі•й–ҖйӮ„жҳҜжҷӮй–“пјҢйҖҷдәӣе°Ҹж–№жі•пјҢеҸӘжҳҜдәӣиғҪе№«еҠ©дҪ еӯёзҝ’жӣҙжңүж•ҲзҺҮзҡ„ж–№ејҸгҖӮ еӯёжңғдёҖеҖӢеӨ–иӘһеҲ°еә•иғҪе№№еҳӣ вҖ” еҝғеҫ—е°Қж–јжҲ‘иҖҢиЁҖпјҢиӘһиЁҖдёҚжҳҜдёҖеҖӢе·Ҙе…·пјҢдёҚжҳҜдёҖеҖӢжҠҖиғҪпјҢд»–жҳҜдёҖеҖӢдәәе’Ңдәәд№Ӣй–“жәқйҖҡиҲҮдәҶи§Јзҡ„ж©ӢжЁ‘пјҢж–ҮеҢ–иӘһиЁҖжҳҜз·Ҡз·ҠзӣёйҖЈзҡ„пјҢеҫҲеӨҡдәәиҠұдәҶеҫҲеӨҡжҷӮй–“еӯёдәҶдёҖзҡ„иӘһиЁҖпјҢдҪҶжҳҜйӮ„жҳҜе§ӢзөӮжІ’жңүдҪҝз”Ёе®ғзҡ„еӢҮж°Ј;жҲ–иҖ…д»–еҖ‘еҚідҫҝдәҶи§ЈйҖҷеҖӢиӘһиЁҖпјҢеҚ»дёҚзҹҘйҒ“иҰҒжҖҺйәје’Ңз•¶ең°дәәжәқйҖҡиө·пјҢжҲ–и·ҹдёҚдёҠд»–еҖ‘иӘӘи©ұзҡ„жӯҘиӘҝиҲҮзҜҖеҘҸгҖӮе”ҜдёҖзҡ„ж–№жі•пјҢеҸӘжңүж”ҫй–ӢиҮӘе·ұеҺ»жҺҘеҸ—дёҚеҗҢзҡ„ж–ҮеҢ–пјҢиө°еҮәиҮӘе·ұзҡ„иҲ’йҒ©еңҲпјҢжҷӮеёёз«ҷеңЁдёҚеҗҢзҡ„и§’еәҰеҺ»жҖқиҖғпјҢдёҰдё”йЎҳж„ҸжҺҘеҸ—дёҚеҗҢзҡ„жғіжі•пјҢдёҚи«–е„Әй»һжҲ–зјәй»һгҖӮиӘһиЁҖеҸҜд»Ҙеё¶дҪ йҖІе…ҘдёҖеҖӢж–°зҡ„ж–ҮеҢ–пјҢдёҰдё”её¶и‘—ж–ҮеҢ–еҮәдҫҶпјҢи·ҹи‘—дҪ жҜҸеҖӢиө°йҒҺзҡ„и¶іи·ЎпјҢе®ғжҳҜдёҖеҖӢиғҪеё¶дҪ жӣҙдәҶи§ЈеҲҘдәәпјҢеҗҢжҷӮд№ҹжӣҙдәҶи§ЈиҮӘе·ұзҡ„дёҖеҖӢж©ӢжЁ‘гҖӮ

entry.title: Kai Fu Lee
entry.content: Kai Fu Lee, ein taiwanischer Informatiker, wurde am 3. Dezember 1961 in Taipeh geboren. Er ist nicht nur ein informatik Wissenschaftler, sodern auch ein erfolgreicher Unternehmer und ein Autor. Lee hat seinem Doktorarbeit Гјber das erste Spracherkenungssystem in der Carnegie Mellon UniversitГӨt absoviert. Danach hat Lee als Manager fГјr Apple, Microsoft und Google gearbeitet. Von 1988 bis 2000, war er an der FГјhrungsposition von Microsoft Research Asia. Ab 2005 war er zum PrГӨsodenten von Google China befГ¶rdert worden. Woxuewang, eine Lernwebseite, wurde von ihm gegrГјndet, um die jungen Chinesen in Bildung und Karrier zu unterstГјtzen. AuГҹerdem schreibt er Artikel und BГјcher Гјber kГјnstliche Intelligenz und Гјber seiner Lebensanschauung. Sein вҖһZehn Briefe an den chinesischen StudentвҖң haben sich im Internet verbreitet. Im Jahr 2013, wurde er mit Kerbs diagnostiziert und hat seine Arbeit fГјr 17 Monate aufgehГ¶rt. 2015 wurde er wieder gesund und ist weiterhin in seiner Arbeit engagiert. Als Inbegriff des Forschers und Pionier der KГјnstlichen Intelligenz, hat er sehr viel MГјhe in die neue Generation gesteckt, damit die Menschen und die KГјnstliche Intelligenz zusammen in der Zukunft gut weiter entwickt werden.

entry.title: Die chinesisische Sprache
entry.content: Die chinesischen Sprachen sind Tonsprachen und entstammen aus der sinotibetischen Sprachfamilie. Heuzutage wurden die chinesischen Sprache von ca.1,5 Millarden Menschen gesprochen, von denen die meisten in China und Taiwan leben. AuГҹerdem ist Mandarin die Hauptsprache mit der grГ¶Гҹten Anzahl (ca.8,89 Millionnen) an Sprechern von allen chinesischen Sprachen und ist auch die Sprache mit den weltweit meisten Muttersprachlern. Es gibt hauptsГӨchlich drei chinesische Dialekte, darunter der Mandarin-Dialekt bzw. Hochchinesisch aus Perking, nordГ¶stliche Dialekt und sГјdliche Dialekt. Jedoch kГ¶nnen die Sprecher von verschiedenen Dialekten sich untereinander nicht verstГӨndigen, aber die WГ¶rter werden in den gleichen chinesischen Schriftzeichen geschrieben. Es gibt insgesamt Гјber 100,000 Schriftzeichen, von denen der Гјberwiegende Teil jedoch heute selten verwenden wird. FГјr den alltГӨglichen Bedarf ist die Kenntnis von 3000 bis 5000 Schriftzeichen ausreichend, um die Zeitungen und die BГјcher zu nachvollziehen. In China wird Manderin mit PД«nyД«n und vereinfachen Chinesisch verwendet und in Taiwan wird es mit ZhГ№yД«n und traditionellen Chinesisch verwenden. Das ГӨlteste Schriftzeichen ist vermutlich vor etwa 3000 Jahren erfunden worden und wurde auf Orakelknochen gezeichnet. ZusГӨtzlich bestehen die Schriftzeichen hauptsГӨchlich aus sechs Kategorien, worunter Piktogramme, Symbole, Ideogramme, Phonogramme, Entlehnungen und Synonyme. Die Гјberwiegende Mehrheit sind Phonogramme mit dem Anteil von 90 Prozent.

entry.title: Das chinesische Neujahrsfest
entry.content: Das chinesische Neujahrsfest ist das wichtigeste Fest in Taiwan. Es findest jeweils an unterschiedlichen Tagen statt, denn das chinesische Neujahr hГӨngt von dem Mondkalender ab. Jedes Jahr bekommt man Ferien von einem Tag vor Silvester bis zum 5.1. von des Monkalenders. Das chinesiches Neujahr ist ein Clan- und Familiefest, weshalb egal wo die Familiemitglieder wohnen, treffen alle durch das Fest zusammen. Es gibtвҖҷs viele Traditionen, die man im Neujahr machen. Am Silvester kommt die Familie zu einem reichhaltingen Essen, traditionell mit Fisch, der wird jedoch nicht vollstГӨndig gegessen. Weil das Wort ein Homophon fГјr вҖһГңberflussвҖң ist, was auch вҖһWohlstandвҖң fГјr das kommende Jahr bedeutet. Nach dem Essen, wird die Familie zusammen spielen, Fernsehen schauen oder sich mit den Leuten unterhalten. AnschlieГҹend, werden die Kinder den roten Umschlag mit Geld von den Verwandten bekommen. AuГҹerdem, viele Leute spielen gerne Mah-Juongg um wenig Geld und auch Lotto, um GlГјck und SpaГҹ in das neue Jahr mitzubringen. Im Neujahr verreist man viel mit der Familie und den Freunden zusammen, sodass die InnenstГӨdte immer voll mit Menschen sind. Das chinesische Neujahrsfest ist ein groГҹartiges Fest, dass welches die Zusammenkunft der Familie und immer eng und stark verbindet.

entry.title: Controling Processes
entry.content: Controling Process Program to Process program exacute -> process Components of Process Address space in mem A set of DS within the kernel Attributes of the Process PID, PPID UID, EUID GID, EGID Niceness Process Lifecycle fork: same PID exac: exacute another programm with diff PID exit: child use _exit to tell kernel that it is ready to end and childвҖҷs parent will know wait: parent use wait to wait for childвҖҷs death Attributes of the process вҖ“ UIDгҖҒGIDгҖҒEUID and EGID The effective uid and gid can be used to enable or restrict the additional permissions Effective uid will be set to: Real uid if setuid bit is off The file ownerвҖҷs uid if setuid bit is on Signal telling a process something has happened Signals can be sent communication between processes kill, interrupt, or suspend process гҖҒ bg, fg administrator to achieve various results kill pid killall process violate the rules divide by zero Illegal memory access Signal вҖ“ Actions when receiving signal Depend on whether there is a designated handler routine for that signal вҖңCatchingвҖқ the signal Two ways to prevent signals from arriving Ignored Blocked FreeBSD signals Signal вҖ“ Send signals: kill %kill [-signal] pid ex: kill -9 pid kill -l killall tcsh killall -u harry Niceness (Nice Value) form -20 ~ +20 (-20 high to +20 low priority) related to CPU time quantum Inherent Property child niceless cant be higher than parent Root has complete freedom in setting nice value other users can only set for lower priority Niceness вҖ“ nice and renice nice format OS nice : $ /usr/bin/nice [range] utility [argument] csh nice(built-in) : $ nice [range] utility [argument] ex:$ nice +10 ps -l renice format cpuset command To see how many CPUs on your machine cpuset -g Run commands with less CPUs cpuset -l cpus cmd Process States ps command ps ps aux ps auxww a:all w:wide top command top -q run top and renice it to -20 top -u donвҖҷt map uid to username top -U show process owned by user htop (advanced top) Runaway process Processes that use up excessive system resource or just go berserk kill -TERM for unknown process renice it to a higher nice value for reasonable process

entry.title: tmux
entry.content: tmuxWhatвҖҷs tmux A terminal multiplexer Allow open multiple tabs (multiple terminals)Why we need tmux Keep your working session alive tmux will not be terminated when connection is lostAdvantage of tmux: Multiple sessions, windows, panes sessions -browser tmux command will open a new session window -tab One session can have multiple windows pane -spit window A window can be split into multiple panesSession Create a new session (open a new browser) $ tmux Outside of tmux session Detach current session $ tmux detach Inside of tmux session Attach to previous session $ tmux attach Attach only if previous sessions exist List opened sessions $ tmux list-sessions Attach to previous session by id $ tmux attach -t session-id tmux - bindkey and command Defualt: Ctrl + b Command: The common command For Pane <Ctrl+b> + "пјҡйҖІиЎҢж°ҙе№іеҲҶеүІ <Ctrl+b> + %пјҡйҖІиЎҢеһӮзӣҙеҲҶеүІ <Ctrl+b> + <ж–№еҗ‘йҚө>пјҡз§»еӢ•еҲ°е…¶д»– pane <Ctrl+b> + xпјҡй—ңй–үзӣ®еүҚзҡ„ pane For Session 1.<Ctrl+b> + dпјҡжҠҠ session ж”ҫеҲ°иғҢжҷҜ(detach) 2.$ tmux atпјҡattach еҲ°иғҢжҷҜзҡ„ session 3.$ tmux attach -t <number>: й–ӢеҲ°жҢҮе®ҡзҡ„session 4.$tmux kill-session [ <number> ]:еҲӘйҷӨжҢҮе®ҡзҡ„session 5.$tmux ls:еұ•зӨәжүҖжңүзҡ„sessions Costimize you tmux create a config file ~/.tmux.conf Look up on Internet for more details tmux v.s. screen

entry.title: Shell Programming
entry.content: #Shell Programming Bourne Shell Change to Bourne Shell (sh) % echo $SHELL % sh -> $ Executable script Shebang #!/bin/sh #!/bin/bash #!/usr/local/bin/bash #!/usr/bin/env bash #!/usr/bin/env python Execution $ sh test.sh $ chmod a+x test.sh $ ./test.sh Shell variables Assignment Access Quotation marks Single quotes(вҖҳxxxвҖҷ): жҠҠиЈЎйқўзҡ„жқұиҘҝз•¶еӯ—дёІзӣҙжҺҘеҚ° Double quotes(вҖңxxxвҖқ): жҠҠиЈЎйқўзҡ„жқұиҘҝз•¶жҲҗspecialзҡ„ и®Ҡж•ёжҲ–е…¶д»–жқұиҘҝеҚ°еҮә Back quotes(xxx): The stdout of the command Shell variable operator вҖңBadвҖқ : var is not set or the value is null вҖңGoodвҖқ : var is set and is not null Predefined shell variables Environment Variables Other useful variables: вҖў $* : all arguments are formed into a long string вҖў $@ : all arguments are formed into separated strings awk йіҘе“Ҙawkж•ҷеӯё awkи®Җfile dir link жӘ”жЎҲйЎһеһӢжңү: d: directories еҸҜз”Ё/^d/дҫҶи®ҖеҸ– -: files еҸҜз”Ё/^-/дҫҶи®ҖеҸ– l: links еҸҜз”Ё/^l/дҫҶи®ҖеҸ–

entry.title: Shells
entry.content: Shells Interface to communicate with kernel Unix Shells еёёиҰӢUnix жҢҮд»Ө: UNIX еёёиҰӢжҢҮд»Өж•ҷеӯё Shell Environment Variables Variables and Strings Quotes Shell Special Characters ex: Built-in Shell Commands Input/Output Redirection File and Directory Related Commands Select and File Processing Related Commands Example usage: Find the occurrence of certain pattern in filegrep -l harry * Print the line number when using grepgrep -n harry /etc/passwd print the processes owned by harryps auxww | grep ^harry List harryвҖҷs id, uid, home, shell in /etc/passwdgrep harry /etc/passwd | cut -f1,3,6,7 -d: Cut out file permission and file name from ls outputls -l | grep -v ^total | cut -c1-11 -c47- -c1-12: 1 st ~12 th characters (start from 1, instead of 0) -c47-:characters after 47 th character (include 47 th) Use awk to generate the same behavior of cutawk -F: '{print $1 " " $6}' /etc/passwd Sortls -al | sort -k 5,5 -r tr -Translate characters Change all alphabet to uppercasetr "A-Z" "a-z" < file1 > file2 Change all character to another charatergrep lctseng /etc/passwd | tr ":" "\nвҖң Delete tab in file1tr -d "\t" < file1 Delete multiple space in file1tr -s " " " " < file1 xargs Command want to ping all hosts in file Command History in (t)csh

entry.title: Der lГӨngste Weg nach Hamburg жј«й•·зҡ„жјўе Ўд№ӢиЎҢ
entry.content: еңЁ2016е№ҙпјҢжҲ‘й«ҳдёӯз•ўжҘӯзҡ„жҡ‘еҒҮпјҢжҲ‘дёҖз•ўжҘӯзҡ„йҡ”еӨ©е°ұжҗӯдёҠдәҶйЈӣж©ҹпјҢеұ•й–Ӣе…©еҖӢжңҲзҡ„еҫ·еңӢж—…иЎҢгҖӮжҲ‘еңЁйҖҷеҖӢе…©еҖӢжңҲжҲ‘еӣһеҺ»жӢңиЁӘжҲ‘2014е№ҙеңЁеҫ·еңӢдәӨжҸӣзҡ„иҪҹе®¶гҖҒд»ҘеҸҠиӘҚиӯҳзҡ„еҫ·еңӢжңӢеҸӢеҖ‘гҖӮжІ’жңүзү№е®ҡзҡ„е®үжҺ’пјҢиЁұеӨҡжӢңиЁӘз”ҡиҮійғҪжҳҜеүҚдёҖйҖұжүҚзўәе®ҡзҡ„пјҢз„¶иҖҢзҘһеҘҮзҡ„жҳҜпјҢеңЁж•ҙи¶ҹж—…йҒҠзҡ„йҒҺзЁӢпјҢйҷӨдәҶеҺ»зЁӢзҡ„зҸӯж©ҹе»¶йҒІд»ҘиҮҙжІ’жҗӯеҲ°еҺҹжң¬е®ҡеҘҪзҡ„зҒ«и»ҠеӨ–пјҢдёҖи·ҜйғҪжҳҜйқһеёёзҡ„й ҶжҡўпјҢж„ҹи¬қе…©еҖӢжңҲзҡ„жҷӮй–“йғҪжңүдәәеҸҜд»ҘжҸҗдҫӣзөҰжҲ‘еҗғдҪҸпјҢи®“жҲ‘еңЁж—…иЎҢдёӯдёҚз”ЁйӨҗйўЁйңІе®ҝгҖӮж—…иЎҢжңҖиҖҗдәәе°Ӣе‘ізҡ„зҡ„ең°ж–№е°ұжҳҜпјҢе®ғзёҪе……ж»ҝи‘—иЁұеӨҡй©ҡеҘҮзҡ„дәәгҖҒдәӢгҖҒзү©пјҢзёҪжңғжңүй җж–ҷд№ӢеӨ–зҡ„дәӢжғ…жңғеңЁдҪ з„ЎеҝғжҸ’жҹіеҫҢпјҢжҲҗзӮәдёҖеҖӢеҖӢзІҫеҪ©ж•…дәӢпјҢйӮЈжҳҜдёҖж®өеҲ°жјўе Ўзҡ„ж—…зЁӢй©ҡеҘҮд№Ӣж—…вҖҰвҖҰ -жјўе Ў йҰ¬дёҒи·Ҝеҫ·ж•ҷе Ӯ дёҖеҖӢжҷҙз©әиҗ¬йҮҢзҡ„дёҠеҚҲпјҢжҲ‘еҫһжҲ‘зҡ„з¬¬дәҢиҪҹе®¶йҷ„иҝ‘зҡ„зҒ«и»Ҡз«ҷ(Wittmund)жә–еӮҷеҮәзҷјеҲ°дёӢдёҖдҪҚжңӢеҸӢе®¶пјҢжҲ‘е’ҢжңӢеҸӢзҙ„еңЁжјўе Ўе…ҲжңғеҗҲпјҢжү“з®—еңЁжјўе ЎйҖӣдёҖйҖӣд№ӢеҫҢеҶҚеӣһеҺ»д»–е®¶(MГ¶lln)гҖӮдёӯйҖ”жңғз¶“йҒҺдәҶе№ҫеҖӢијғеӨ§зҡ„еҹҺеёӮйҖІиЎҢиҪүи»ҠпјҢз•¶жҲ‘еңЁеҘ§зҷ»е Ў(Oldenburg)иҪүи»ҠпјҢдёҖдёҠи»ҠпјҢжҲ‘й–Ӣе§Ӣе°Ӣжүҫеә§дҪҚпјҢеңЁж»ҝијүзҡ„и»Ҡе»ӮдёӯпјҢжҲ‘еҘҪдёҚе®№жҳ“жүҫеҲ°дёҖеҖӢз©әдҪҚпјҢз•¶жӯЈжҲ‘иҰҒеқҗдёӢдҫҶжҷӮпјҢжңүдёҖдҪҚз©ҝи‘—еҘҮиЈқз•°жңҚзҡ„з”·дәәпјҢжүӢжҢҒдёҖжқҜshotжқҜеӯҗпјҢжҠҠжҲ‘ж””дәҶдёӢдҫҶвҖҰвҖҰ д»–з”Ёи‘—еҫҲеҫ·ж–Үи…”иӘҝзҡ„иӢұж–ҮпјҢй–ӢзҺ©з¬‘ең°и·ҹжҲ‘иӘӘ:гҖҢеҰӮжһңдҪ жғіеқҗеңЁйҖҷйӮҠпјҢдҪ иҰҒе…ҲжҠҠйҖҷжҠҠйҖҷеҖӢshotд№ҫжҺүгҖҚпјҢж®ҠдёҚзҹҘпјҢжҲ‘з”Ёи‘—жөҒеҲ©зҡ„еҫ·ж–Үеӣһд»–:гҖҢз•¶з„¶пјҢзӮәд»ҖйәјдёҚе‘ў?гҖҚпјҢжҲ‘д»Җйәјд№ҹжІ’еӨҡжғіпјҢе°ұдёҖеҸЈд№ҫжҺүдәҶshotпјҢйҡЁеҫҢжҲ‘д»”зҙ°дёҖзңӢпјҢеҺҹдҫҶз©ҝи‘—йҳҝжӢүдёҒзҡ„иЎЈжңҚпјҢд»–е’Ңд»–зҡ„жңӢеҸӢеҖ‘жӯЈеңЁиҫҰе‘ҠеҲҘе–®иә«и¶ҙпјҢд»–еҖ‘йғҪеҫҲй©ҡиЁқзҡ„е•ҸжҲ‘жҖҺйәјжңғеҫ·ж–ҮпјҢеңЁеҫ·еңӢеҒҡд»Җйәј? жҲ‘й–Ӣе§Ӣи·ҹд»–еҖ‘иҒҠиө·жҲ‘зҡ„дәӨжҸӣз”ҹжҙ»пјҢй–’и«Үд№ҹиҒҠеҲ°дәҶжҲ‘еҖ‘еңЁеҫ·еңӢдҪҸзҡ„ең°ж–№пјҢжҲ‘и·ҹд»–иӘӘжҲ‘дҪҸеңЁWittmund(еңЁеҫ·еңӢиҘҝеҢ—и§’зҡ„жө·еІёйҷ„иҝ‘жңүдёҖеҖӢең°еҚҖеҸ«еҒҡOstfrieslandпјҢWittmundд№ҹеңЁе…¶дёӯ)пјҢжҲ‘и·ҹд»–иӘӘжҲ‘йғҪжҲІзЁұ(Ostfriesland)жҳҜдё–з•Ңзҡ„еұҒзңј(Arsch der Welt)пјҢеӣ зӮәйӮЈең°иҷ•еҒҸеғ»дәӨйҖҡијғдёҚдҫҝд№ҹжҜ”ијғе°‘дәәеҸЈеұ…дҪҸпјҢиӘӘеҲ°йҖҷйӮҠд»–еҖ‘еҘҪдјјеҫҲжңүе…ұйіҙеӣ зӮәд»–еҖ‘жӯЈеҘҪд№ҹжҳҜдҫҶиҮӘйҖҷеҖӢең°еҚҖпјҢжӯӨжҷӮд»–еҸҲй–ӢдёҖз“¶е•Өй…’пјҢдҫҶйҖҷжқҜж•¬дҪ пјҢжҲ‘е°ұжҒӯж•¬дёҚеҰӮеҫһе‘ҪпјҢе–қдәҶгҖӮ жҺҘи‘—жҲ‘еҖ‘еҸҲиҒҠеҲ°пјҢиҰҒеҺ»зҡ„зӣ®зҡ„ең°пјҢеҺҹдҫҶе®ғеҖ‘жӯЈиҰҒеҺ»жјўе ЎйҖЈй–Ӣе…©еӨ©зҡ„и¶ҙпјҢз•¶жҲ‘и·ҹд»–еҖ‘иӘӘжҲ‘д№ҹиҰҒеҺ»жјўе ЎжүҫжңӢеҸӢжҷӮпјҢд»–еҖ‘жҜ«дёҚзҢ¶иұ«ең°йӮҖи«ӢдәҶжҲ‘е’Ңе®ғеҖ‘дёҖиө·дёҠи·ҜпјҢе°ұйҖҷжЁЈзҙ жҳ§е№із”ҹпјҢдёҖеҖӢ18жӯІзҡ„дәһжҙІз”·еӯ©пјҢе’Ңд»–еҖ‘дёҖзҫӨйғҪеҝ«30жӯІзҡ„еҫ·еңӢйқ’е№ҙпјҢиёҸдёҠдәҶдёҖи·Ҝе–қеҲ°жјўе Ўзҡ„еҲ—и»ҠгҖӮйӮЈеҖӢжә–еӮҷзөҗе©ҡзҡ„жә–ж–°йғҺпјҢз©ҝи‘—дёҖиә«зҡ„йҳҝжӢүдёҒиЈқпјҢиӨІеӯҗдёҠжҺӣи‘—дёҖзӣһеӨ§зҘһзҮҲпјҢжҲ‘еҖ‘йқ и‘—д»–еңЁи»Ҡе»Ӯе…§йҒҠиө°пјҢе°ӢжүҫжғіиҰҒиЁұйЎҳзҡ„д№ҳе®ўпјҢи«Ӣд»–еҖ‘ж‘©ж“ҰзҘһзҮҲпјҢдёҰеёҢжңӣеҸҜд»Ҙе№«еҠ©д»–еҖ‘е®ҢжҲҗд»–еҖ‘зҡ„еҝғйЎҳпјҢжҲ‘еҖ‘еңЁи·ҜдёҠпјҢжҺҘж”¶еҲ°иЁұеӨҡдәәзҡ„иЁұйЎҳпјҢзҘқзҰҸи‘—ж–°йғҺе’Ңж–°еЁҳе©ҡеҫҢз”ҹжҙ»зҫҺж»ҝгҖҒзҷҪй ӯеҒ•иҖҒпјҢд№ҹзҘқзҰҸжҲ‘еҖ‘еңЁжјўе ЎжңүдёҖж®өзҫҺеҘҪзҡ„ж—…зЁӢпјҢжҲ‘еҖ‘д№ҹиҮӘе·ұж‘©ж“ҰпјҢеёҢжңӣжӣҙеӨҡи·ҜдәәйҷӘжҲ‘еҖ‘дёҖиө·е–қй»һе°Ҹй…’пјҢдә«еҸ—и‘—йҖҷзҳӢзӢӮдё”еҝ«жЁӮзҡ„жҷӮе…үпјҢе•Өй…’дёҖз“¶жҺҘи‘—дёҖз“¶пјҢshotдёҖжқҜжҺҘи‘—дёҖжқҜпјҢжҲ‘еҖ‘йҒҮеҲ°дәҶеҫҲеӨҡзҶұжғ…зҡ„и·ҜдәәпјҢд№ҹйҒҮеҲ°дәҶеҫҲchillзҡ„и»Ҡй•·пјҢд»–еңЁи·ҜйҒҺй©—зҘЁжҷӮпјҢдёҚдҪҶжІ’жңүжҺғжҲ‘еҖ‘зҡ„иҲҲпјҢйӮ„йҷӘдәҶжҲ‘еҖ‘дёҖиө·иҒҠиҒҠеӨ©гҖҒиЁұйЎҳпјҢиҖҢжҳҜеҫҲиҰӘеҲҮең°и«ӢжҲ‘еҖ‘иө°еҲ°е…¶д»–дәәжҜ”ијғе°‘зҡ„и»Ҡе»ӮпјҢеҘҪи®“жҲ‘еҖ‘еҸҜд»ҘзҺ©еҫ—жӣҙзӣЎиҲҲдё”дёҚеҪұйҹҝеҲ°еҲҘдәәгҖӮ - жјўе Ў зҒ«и»Ҡз«ҷ е°ұйҖҷжЁЈдёҖи·ҜдёҚзҹҘйҒ“з¶“йҒҺдәҶе№ҫж¬ЎиҪүи»ҠпјҢиө°йҒҺдәҶе№ҫзҜҖи»Ҡе»ӮпјҢе–қйҒҺдәҶеӨҡе°‘е•Өй…’гҖҒзғҲй…’пјҢжҲ‘еҖ‘жҗ–жҗ–жҷғжҷғзҡ„еңЁжјўе Ўзҷ»йҷёдәҶпјҢйҖҷж„ҹиҰәеҘҪеғҸеүӣеҫһиҲ№дёҠдёӢиҲ№жҷӮзҡ„ж„ҹиҰәпјҢеҘҪеғҸж•ҙеҖӢең°зҗғйғҪзӮәдҪ иҪүеӢ•пјҢжҲ‘жІ’жңүйҶүпјҢеҸӘжҳҜйҖҷи¶ҹж—…иЎҢе°ҚжҲ‘дҫҶиӘӘзңҹзҡ„жҳҜеӨӘй…·гҖҒеӨӘй–ӢеҝғдәҶгҖӮдёҖеҖӢдёҚз¶“ж„Ҹзҡ„жұәе®ҡпјҢи®“жҲ‘иёҸдёҠдәҶйҖҷеҖӢжңүи¶Јзҡ„ж—…зЁӢпјҢжҲ–иЁұзҙ жҳ§е№із”ҹгҖҒжҲ–иЁұдёҚеҗҢзЁ®ж—ҸгҖҒе№ҙйҪЎпјҢеҸӘиҰҒжңүе…ұйҖҡзҡ„еӣһжҶ¶гҖҒйӮ„жңүй…’зІҫзҡ„еӮ¬еҢ–пјҢдёҖеҲҮйғҪжҳҜжңүеҸҜиғҪзҡ„гҖӮйҶүеҫҢеӨ§дёҲеӨ«пјҢжҲ‘иЈқд»ҖйәјдәӢйғҪжІ’зҷјз”ҹпјҢе°ұйҖҷжЁЈиёҸдёҠеҺ»жүҫжҲ‘жңӢеҸӢзҡ„и·ҜдёҠпјҢйӮЈжҷӮжүҚдёӯеҚҲдёҖй»һпјҢдёҖеӨ©йӮ„еҫҲжј«й•·пјҢеҘҮе№»д№Ӣж—…жүҚжӯЈиҰҒй–Ӣе§ӢвҖҰвҖҰ

entry.title: Run APM code on Docker
entry.content: Run the APM code on dockerPrerequisite docker installed1sudo apt install docker.io Build the 16.04 ubuntu docker image Go the directory of docker/addm-build-ubuntu16 and run the build.sh file 12cd docker/addm-build-ubuntu16bash build.sh Problem may encounter: E:Unable to locate package ca-certificates The command '/bin/sh -c apt-get install -y ca-certificates' return a non-zero code:100 Solution: Set the DNS server to ITRI Domain as below This problem may also happened in docker container later, be sure to change the nameserverRun the image on docker and build the code cd .. to the docker directory and change the image source in run-docker.sh (ex:u16.04) and run the run-docker.sh file with bash 1bash run-docker.sh Problem may encounter: Permission denied of XXX.sh Solution: Use command chmod +x ./sh to make the .sh file executable After entering the container, go to the directory addm and run the build.sh file and the result will be as the photo below12cd addmbash build.sh The result file package.tar.gz After building the code, we will get a package.tar.gz file which will be located on the addm-master directory on the host as the photo shows Change the file kvm.ko and kvm-intel.ko in the kernel modules untar the package.tar.gz we will get a addm directory The kvm.ko and kvm-intel.ko, built by us, are under the directory /addm/bin Move these two files to the kernel module in the /lib/module/4.15.0-50-generic/kernel/arch/x86/kvm Reboot to check whether the system function well Run and test at least one VM on the host to check the KVM modules are working.

entry.title: Web Server & API Server
entry.content: Web Server and API ServerWeb Container Webе®№еҷЁжҳҜдёҖзЁ®жңҚеӢҷиӘҝз”Ёзҡ„иҰҸзҜ„пјҢе…¶дёӯжңүWeb Containerе’ҢEJB Containerзӯү Webе®№еҷЁзөҰиҷ•ж–је…¶дёӯзҡ„жҮүз”ЁзЁӢеәҸзө„д»¶пјҲJSPгҖҒServletпјүжҸҗдҫӣз’°еўғпјҢJSPгҖҒServletзӣҙжҺҘиҲҮе®№еҷЁдёӯзҡ„з’°еўғи®ҠйҮҸжү“дәӨйҒ“пјҢиҖҢдёҚеҝ…й—ңжіЁе…¶д»–зі»зөұе•ҸйЎҢ Webе®№еҷЁжӣҙеӨҡзҡ„жҳҜиҲҮHTTPзҡ„и«ӢжұӮжү“дәӨйҒ“пјҢиҖҢEJBе®№еҷЁдёҚжҳҜпјҢе®ғжӣҙеӨҡзҡ„жҳҜи·ҹж•ёж“ҡеә«е…¶д»–жңҚеӢҷжү“дәӨйҒ“ Web Server WebжңҚеӢҷеҷЁеҸҜд»Ҙиҷ•зҗҶHTTPеҚ”иӯ°пјҢз•¶WebжңҚеӢҷеҷЁж”¶еҲ°дёҖеҖӢHTTPи«ӢжұӮпјҢе°ұжңғиҝ”еӣһдёҖеҖӢHTTPйҹҝжҮүгҖӮ Apache Server:жҳҜз°Ўе–®зҡ„HTTPжңҚеӢҷеҷЁпјҢйҖҹеәҰеҝ«пјҢеңЁеӨ§еһӢз¶Із«ҷдёӯпјҢеёёиҲҮжҮүз”ЁжңҚеӢҷеҷЁй…ҚеҗҲйғЁзҪІпјҢеғ…ж”ҜжҢҒйқңж…Ӣй ҒйқўгҖӮ Apache Nginx: и‘—еҗҚзҡ„HTTPжңҚеӢҷеҷЁпјҢйҖҹеәҰеҝ«пјҢж”ҜжҢҒиІ ијүеқҮиЎЎгҖӮ client-server жһ¶ж§Ӣ: Clientз«ҜеҸҜд»ҘжғіеғҸжҲҗжҳҜдёҖеҖӢдё»еӢ•зҡ„и§’иүІпјҢе®ғжңғзҷјйҖҒи«ӢжұӮ(request)зөҰserverз«ҜпјҢдёҰй–Ӣе§Ӣзӯүеҫ…зӣҙеҲ°ж”¶еҲ°еӣһжҮүгҖӮ Serverз«ҜеҸҜд»ҘжғіеғҸжҲҗжҳҜдёҖеҖӢиў«еӢ•зҡ„и§’иүІпјҢе®ғжңғзӯүеҲ°clientз«ҜзҷјйҖҒи«ӢжұӮ(request)дҫҶеҫҢй–Ӣе§Ӣиҷ•зҗҶпјҢдёҰеӣһеӮідёҖеҖӢеӣһжҮү(response)зөҰclientз«ҜгҖӮ P2Pжһ¶ж§Ӣ: AеҲҶдә«дёҖеҖӢжӘ”жЎҲдёҠеҺ»пјҢз¶“hash function еҫҢз”ўз”ҹдёҖзө„keyпјҢclientе°ұеҸҜйҖҸйҒҺйҖҷеҖӢkey дҫҶaccess еҲ°AеҲҶдә«зҡ„жӘ”жЎҲ Aplication Server дҪңзӮәжҮүз”ЁжңҚеӢҷеҷЁпјҢиҰҒжұӮеҸҜд»ҘйҖҡйҒҺеҗ„зЁ®еҚ”иӯ°пјҲеҢ…жӢ¬HTTPеҚ”иӯ°пјүжҠҠе•ҶжҘӯйӮҸијҜжҡҙйңІзөҰе®ўжҲ¶з«ҜжҮүз”ЁзЁӢеәҸпјҢжү®жј”дёҖеҖӢеҲҶе·Ҙз®ЎзҗҶзҡ„и…іиүІ Tomcat: Tomcatеғ…жҳҜеҖӢHTTPжңҚеӢҷеҷЁпјҢжҳҜApacheйјҺеҠӣж”ҜжҢҒзҡ„Java WebжңҚеӢҷеҷЁпјҢжҳҜServlet/JSP APIзҡ„еҹ·иЎҢеҷЁпјҢд№ҹе°ұжҳҜжүҖи¬Ӯзҡ„Servletе®№еҷЁпјҢдёҚж”ҜжҢҒEJBгҖҒJMSгҖӮзӣёе°Қж–јJBOSSдҫҶиӘӘијғзӮәиј•йҮҸзҙҡпјҢеҜҰзҸҫзҡ„еҠҹиғҪд№ҹијғе°‘пјҢдёҚиғҪе®Ңж•ҙзҡ„ж”ҜжҢҒжңҖж–°зҡ„JavaEEжЁҷжә–пјҢйҒ©з”Ёж–јијғзӮәз°Ўе–®зҡ„WebжҮүз”Ё JBOSS: JBOSSжҳҜй–ӢжәҗJava EEжҮүз”ЁжңҚеӢҷеҷЁпјҢе®ғдёҚдҪҶжҳҜServletе®№еҷЁпјҢиҖҢдё”жҳҜEJBе®№еҷЁпјҢе®ғжҸҗдҫӣдәҶжӣҙеӨҡй–Ӣз®ұеҚіз”Ёзҡ„еҠҹиғҪзү№жҖ§пјҢд№ҹжӣҙйҮҚпјҢе®ғе®Ңж•ҙзҡ„еҜҰзҸҫдәҶJ2EE 6гҖӮ Glassfish: GlassfishжҳҜOracle й–Ӣзҷјзҡ„е®ҳж–№Java EEе®№еҷЁпјҢд№ҹжҳҜеҗҢжҷӮж”ҜжҢҒServletе’ҢEJBпјҢж”ҜжҢҒжңҖж–°зҡ„зү№жҖ§пјҢжңүиҮӘе·ұзҡ„webе®№еҷЁпјҢж”ҜжҢҒйӣҶзҫӨпјҢж”ҜжҢҒзҶұйғЁзҪІгҖӮ Restful Web APIWhy we need Restful API The cons of traditional web service: Data and presentation logic are bund together, which make it difficult to debug and test. Either the change of Data or the presentation logic will have cause the other to change as well. Thus the cache system doesnвҖҷt work Intergration of Data and presentation logic will bring the heavy load for servers жүҖд»Ҙе°ҮDataи·ҹPresentation Logicеҫ№еә•еҲҮй–ӢпјҢServerдёҖеҲҶзӮәдәҢжҲҗеүҚз«ҜWebи·ҹеҫҢз«ҜAPI Pros: Web ServerеҸҜд»ҘйҖҸйҒҺеҝ«еҸ–ж©ҹеҲ¶е°ҮиіҮж–ҷеӯҳеңЁWeb Serverз«ҜпјҢеҰӮжһңиіҮж–ҷжІ’жңүж”№пјҢе°ұеҸӘй Ҳеҹ·иЎҢ presentation logic зҡ„йғЁеҲҶ е°ұеҸҜд»ҘеӮізөҰclientдәҶпјҢжңүж•Ҳзҡ„йҷҚдҪҺserverиІ ж“” Clientз«Ҝд№ҹжңүд»–зҡ„еҝ«еҸ–ж©ҹеҲ¶пјҢеҸӘиҰҒеүҚз«Ҝзҡ„иЁӯиЁҲжІ’жңүж”№йҒҺпјҢpresentation logicдёҚи®Ҡзҡ„жғ…жіҒдёӢпјҢclientз«Ҝд№ҹжІ’еҝ…иҰҒзҷјйҮҚзҷјrequestзөҰWeb ServerпјҢPresentationдёҚз”ЁйҮҚеӮіпјҢзҜҖзңҒз¶Іи·ҜжөҒйҮҸ й–Ӣзҷјж–№йқўд№ҹиғҪз©©е®ҡиЁұеӨҡпјҢеүҚеҫҢз«ҜйӮҸијҜиҒ·иІ¬еҠғеҲҶжё…жҘҡдёҚдә’зӣёеҪұйҹҝпјҢдёҰдё”еңЁеҫҢжңҹй–ӢзҷјдёҠпјҢApi Serverд№ҹйӮ„иғҪйҮҚиӨҮзөҰдёҚеҗҢзҡ„е№іеҸ°дҪҝз”ЁпјҢжҸҗй«ҳзі»зөұзҡ„ж“ҙејөжҖ§ Restful API(Representational State Transfer):жҳҜдёҖзЁ®APIзҡ„иЁӯиЁҲйўЁж јпјҢиЎЁеҫөзӢҖж…Ӣзҡ„еҲҮжҸӣпјҢиЎЁеҫөжҢҮзҡ„е°ұжҳҜеүҚз«Ҝз¶Іи·Ҝд»Ӣйқў(HTML)пјҢзӢҖж…ӢеҲҮжҸӣжҳҜжҢҮдҪҝз”ЁиҖ…еҰӮдҪ•иҲҮиЎЁеҫөдә’еӢ•пјҢз•¶иЎЁеҫөзҷјзҸҫж”№и®ҠпјҢWeb serverе°ұиҰҒе’ҢAPI server дә’еӢ• Restful API pros: з°Ўе–®дё”зөұдёҖзҡ„ApiжҺҘеҸЈпјҢи®“дёҚеҗҢзҡ„еүҚз«ҜServerиғҪеӨ жӣҙж–№дҫҝзҡ„иҲҮеҫҢз«ҜApiе°ҚжҺҘпјҢдёҰдё”жҜҸдёҖзЁ®urlжҺҘеҸЈе°ұд»ЈиЎЁдёҖзЁ®dataпјҢйҷҚдҪҺдәҶзі»зөұиіҮж–ҷд№Ӣй–“зҡ„иҖҰеҗҲжҖ§пјҢи®“еҫҢз«Ҝapiй–ӢзҷјдёҠеҘҪз¶ӯиӯ·дё”еҪҲжҖ§иЁұеӨҡ Resful API cons: дҪҺиҖҰеҗҲе°ұд»ЈиЎЁи‘—й«ҳй¬Ҷж•ЈпјҢз•¶еүҚз«ҜWebзҡ„Presentation LogicйҒҺж–јиӨҮйӣңжҷӮпјҢдҫӢеҰӮдёҖеҖӢй Ғйқў(иЎЁеҫө)йңҖиҰҒеӨҡзЁ®иіҮж–ҷжҷӮпјҢе°ұеҝ…й ҲеӨҡзҷје№ҫеҖӢrequestеҗ‘еҫҢз«ҜиҰҒиіҮж–ҷпјҢдҫӢеҰӮдёҖеҖӢйғЁиҗҪж јй ҒйқўйҷӨдәҶиҰҒж–Үз« е…§е®№йӮ„иҰҒдҪңиҖ…иіҮж–ҷжҷӮпјҢе°ұеҝ…й ҲдёҖж¬Ўзҷје…©еҖӢrequestпјҢдёҖж—ҰPresentation LogicиӨҮйӣңиө·дҫҶпјҢе°ұжңғеӨ§еӨ§еҪұйҹҝзі»зөұзҡ„ж•ҲиғҪгҖӮ

entry.title: Recompile Kernel on Linux
entry.content: Recompile Kernel on LinuxPrerequisite of Building the Kernel Check gcc is installed Tools needed:1sudo apt-get install build-essential libncurses5-dev xz-utils libssl-dev kernel-package wget Step1.0 :еҲ° Kernel.org дёӢијүйңҖиҰҒзҡ„зүҲжң¬ з”Ё git clone жҲ– wget дёӢијүеҺҹе§Ӣзўј дёҰдёҹеҲ° /usr/src зҡ„иіҮж–ҷеӨҫдёӢ123wget https://github.com/torvalds/linux/archive/v4.x/linux-4.15.tar.gz tar zxvf linux-4.15.tar.gzcd linux-4.15/ Step1.1 :Ubuntu Launchpad the Sub-version of Kernel Source CodeLink:https://launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux/4.15.0-50.54 There are 3 files to be downloaded linux_4.15.0.orig.tar.gz & linux_4.15.0-50.54.diff.gz & linux_4.15.0-50.54.dsc .orig.tar.gz -> The original kernel .diff.gz -> The diffence (patch) between the updated code and the original code. .dsc -> A short textfile descripts of the .orig and .diff and connect the files above Use the dpkg to extract the files1$ dpkg-source -x package.dsc Step2: иЁӯе®ҡ config дҫҶиӘҝж•ҙзЎ¬й«”йңҖжұӮ иӨҮиЈҪеҺҹжңүзҡ„ config жӘ”жЎҲпјҢйҮҚж–°еӯҳжҲҗдёҖд»Ҫ .config еҸҜз”Ёxconfig,or menuconfig дҫҶиЁӯе®ҡconfigжӘ” Change the name of compile kernel version name:vim Makefileto change the version name as the photo below before compiling 12cp /boot/config-4.4.0-21-generic ./.configmake menuconfig Step3: Compile the kernel -j 4 д»ЈиЎЁдҪҝз”Ё4ж ёеҝғйҖІиЎҢз·ЁиӯҜпјҢеҸҜд»ҘеҠ йҖҹ compile зҡ„ж…ўй•·йҒҺзЁӢ 12345sudo make -j 4 cleansudo make -j 4sudo make modules -j 4sudo make modules_installsudo make install Step4: жӣҙж–°initramfs image1sudo update-initramfs -u -k all Step5: жӣҙж–°grub-list йҮҚй–Ӣж©ҹеҫҢжҢү Esc й–Ӣж©ҹйҒёе–®е°ұеҸҜзңӢеҲ°еҸҜйҒёзҡ„ж–°иЈқеҘҪзҡ„kernelй–Ӣж©ҹ1sudo update-grub й–Ӣж©ҹеҫҢз”ЁжҢҮе®ҡзүҲжң¬Kernelй–Ӣж©ҹ Edit string in /etc/default/grub, then save1234567shell > sudo vim /etc/default/grub/*edit fromGRUB_DEFAULT=0save toGRUB_DEFAULT=saved*/ жҹҘзңӢзӣ®еүҚй–Ӣж©ҹзҡ„kernel modules1shell > awk -F\' '$1=="menuentry " || $1=="submenu " {print i++ " : " $2}; /\tmenuentry / {print "\t" i-1">"j++ " : " $2};' /boot/grub/grub.cfg йҒёж“ҮжҢҮе®ҡзҡ„kernel зүҲжң¬дёҰдёӢд»ҘдёӢжҢҮд»Ө, д»ҘдёӢжҳҜд»ҘжғіиҰҒдҪҝз”ЁвҖқ4.4.0-87вҖқ(вҖҳ1>4вҖҷ)зӮәдҫӢ123shell > sudo grub-set-default '1>4'shell > sudo update-grubshell > sudo reboot Ubuntu package of Kernel (compiled)1sudo apt-get linux-header-4.15.0-55-generic

entry.title: APM-Deployment
entry.content: title: APM Deploymenttags: Intern, APM description:APM DeploymentAPM structureHaas Machine Machine in 78 Lab Operating Environment Template Host OS: Ubuntu Desktop 16.04 LTS Guest(VM) OS: Ubuntu Desktop 16.04 LTS(CN)(kernel 4.15.0-55) & 14.04 LTS (Test VM)(kernel 4.4.0-87) All of Login Account (Account Type): test (Administrator) All of Login Password: test Additional Installed Packages: vim, openssh-server, desktop sharing, folder sharing Install and Run KVM Install the packages of KVM, then reboot1shell > sudo apt install qemu-kvm libvirt-bin bridge-utils virt-manager Passwordless for Root Edit the file /etc/sudoers12345sudo vim /etc/sudoers##Add the line in the this file username ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL Setup Passwordless with ssh Set up passwordless connection between (CN&SN),(SN & TestVM) and (DN & CN) bidirectionally Set up: PermitRootLogin yes and ssh restart 123sudo vim /etc/ssh/sshd_configsudo service ssh restartsudo service ssh status Change Root password 1234567sudo passwd root``` - ssh-keygen in root and ssh-copy-id to the target - test <-> test - test <-> root - root <-> test - root <-> root ssh-keygen Enter to the endssh-copy-id root@target_ip enter root-password1234- Root й–“жңүжҲҗеҠҹеҒҡеҲ°passwordless еүҮ userй–“е°ұд№ҹжңүpasswordless## Change to the Correct Kernel Version- Use follow commmand to check your OS & kernel infomation lsb_release -a uname -a1- Install 4.15.0-55 kernel image, headers & modules-extra and update initramfs & grub, then reboot shell > sudo apt install linux-image-4.15.0-55-genericshell > sudo apt install linux-headers-4.15.0-55-genericshell > sudo apt install linux-modules-extra-4.15.0-55-genericshell > sudo update-initramfs -u -k allshell > sudo update-grubshell > sudo reboot1- Install 4.4.0-87 kernel image, image-extra & headers and update initramfs & grub, then reboot shell > sudo apt-get install linux-image-4.4.0-87-genericshell > sudo apt-get install linux-image-extra-4.4.0-87-genericshell > sudo apt-get install linux-headers-4.4.0-87-genericshell > sudo update-initramfs -u -k allshell > sudo update-grubshell > sudo reboot12- Edit string in /etc/default/grub, then save /edit fromGRUB_DEFAULT=0save toGRUB_DEFAULT=saved / shell > sudo vim /etc/default/grubshell > awk -F\вҖҷ вҖҳ$1==вҖқmenuentry вҖң || $1==вҖқsubmenu вҖң {print i++ вҖң : вҖң $2}; /\tmenuentry / {print вҖң\tвҖқ i-1вҖқ>вҖқj++ вҖң : вҖң $2};вҖҷ /boot/grub/grub.cfg Select the kernel version you want toshell > sudo grub-set-default вҖҳ1>2вҖҷshell > sudo update-grub123456789101112## Settings Before Running APM Code- Change the path in `_amd.conf` in apm-kernel_4.15.0-55-generic/am1/amdeploy![](https://i.imgur.com/L8BZebC.png)- Change the deployed info in `_amnodespec.yaml` in apm-kernel_4.15.0-55-generic/am1/amdeploy![](https://i.imgur.com/Y576D14.png) - The example above shows 1 compute-node with 2 test-VM and 1 server node![](https://i.imgur.com/I2OeBJl.png) - The vm name must be the same as it in your kvm - Server-node should have java8 installed add repository to the apt-getsudo add-apt-repository ppa:openjdk-r/ppasudo apt-get update install openjdk-8sudo apt-get install openjdk-8-jdk123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536- Change the file `install-no-kvm.sh` in apm-kernel_4.15.0-55-generic/- `sudo su` in root and use `./amd11` to run the code ## Set /etc/hosts- Add В«addmВ»(server node ip) to /etc/hosts on both compute node and server side e.g.: 192.168.100.90 addm- add compute node to /etc/hosts with the same name as in _amnodspec.yaml on server node e.g.: 192.168.100.91 addmcompute1![](https://i.imgur.com/wvYfXxP.png)![](https://i.imgur.com/j2wUsN8.png)## testing if APM is working correctly:1. Make sure mac and ip address of the VM on the compute node is correct2. Install apache webserver on the VM (sudo apt-get install apache2)3. On the compute node run /opt/addm/bin/start-agent4. On the server node run /opt/addm/bin/start-server5. On the server node in a separate terminal run /opt/addm/bin/cli enable 16. Issue http request to the vm (e.g. curl VM_IP). This has to be done from any host except Compute Node, otherwise it will not be trapped.## Web Server Deployment### Environment and Prerequisite- Ubuntu 14.04- Install JDK7```bash=sudo add-apt-repository ppa:openjdk-r/ppa sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install openjdk-7-jdk Install MySQL е®үиЈқйҒҺзЁӢжңғиҰҒдҪ ијёе…Ҙrootеёіиҷҹзҡ„еҜҶзўј1sudo apt-get install mysql-server MySQL Setting Enter MySQL 1shell> mysql Create DB 1mysql> create database apmdb; Import DB 1shell> mysql -u root -p apmdb < /home/test/iii/apm-kernel_4.15.0-55-generic/addmui/DBSchema/apm_v0.1.sql Add one вҖҳMySQLвҖҷ user вҖңusername = test, password = testвҖқ 12mysql> create user 'test'@'%' identified by 'test';mysql> grant all privileges on *.* to 'test'@'%' with grant option; Edit /etc/mysql/mysql.conf.d/mysqld.cnf 12replace: from bind-address = 127.0.0.1 to bind-address = 0.0.0.0 Restart MySQL service 1shell> service mysql restart Install Glassfish е®ҳз¶ІдёӢијүglassfish.zip mysql-connector-java-5.1.35-bin.jarпјҢжҠҠе®ғcopyеҲ°glassfish3/glassfish/lib/ иЈЎйқў ж”№glassfish з®ЎзҗҶе“ЎеҜҶзўјпјҢй җиЁӯеёіиҷҹзӮә adminпјҢеӣ е°ҡжңӘиЁӯе®ҡеҜҶзўјпјҢеңЁжҸҗзӨәEnter admin password> жҷӮпјҢи«ӢзӣҙжҺҘжҢүдёӢenterйҚөпјҢжҺҘдёӢдҫҶеҶҚиЁӯе®ҡж–°зҡ„еҜҶзўјгҖӮ е•ҹеӢ•glassfish иЁӯе®ҡе…ҒиЁұйҒ з«Ҝе®үе…Ёз®ЎзҗҶпјҢе•ҹеӢ•glassfishеҫҢпјҢеҹ·иЎҢд»ҘдёӢжҢҮд»ӨпјҢдёҰйҮҚе•ҹglassfish12345678910111213#1wget http://download.java.net/glassfish/3.1.2.2/release/glassfish-3.1.2.2.zipunzip glassfish-3.1.2.2.zip#2cp ~/mysql-connector-java-5.1.35/mysql-connector-java-5.1.35-bin.jar ~/glassfish3/glassfish/lib/#3~/glassfish3/bin/asadmin change-admin-password#4cd ~/glassfish3/bin./asadmin start-domain#5./asadmin --host localhost --port 4848 enable-secure-admin./asadmin restart-domain иЁӯе®ҡSQL DB Connection Pool Glassfishе•ҹеӢ•еҫҢпјҢжү“й–ӢзҖҸиҰҪеҷЁйҖЈеҲ°https://localhost:4848 й»һйҒёJDBC й»һйҒёJDBC Connection Pools й»һйҒёNewвҖҰ Pool NameеҸҜд»ҘйҡЁдҫҝдҪ еҸ–пјҢе»әиӯ°жңүAPMеӯ—зңјж–№дҫҝиҮӘе·ұиӯҳеҲҘпјҢеҰӮAPMPoolгҖӮResource Typeй»һйҒёjavax.sql.DataSourceгҖӮDatabase Driver Vendorй»һйҒёMySQLгҖӮијёе…Ҙе®ҢеҫҢй»һвҖқNextвҖқжҢүйҲ•гҖӮ еҗ‘дёӢжҚІеӢ•еҸҜзңӢеҲ°Additional PropertiesеҲ—иЎЁијёе…Ҙж¬„дҪҚеҰӮдёӢжүҖзӨәиЎЁ 3: ж–°еўһacs JDBC Connection Pool Step2 Additional Propertiesијёе…Ҙж¬„дҪҚ Property Value еӮҷиЁ» User root ServerName localhost иҰҒйҖЈеҲ°зҡ„MySQL serverзҡ„hostnameпјҢе®үиЈқеңЁеҗҢдёҖеҸ°еЎ«localhost DatabaseName apmdb Password password и«Ӣијёе…Ҙз•¶еҲқе®үиЈқMySQLжҷӮжүҖиЁӯе®ҡзҡ„rootзҡ„еҜҶзўј еҶҚдҫҶеӢҫйҒёUrlи·ҹURLеҫҢй»һвҖқDelete PropertiesвҖқжҢүйҲ•гҖӮйЎҚеӨ–еҶҚиЁӯдёҖеҖӢж¬„дҪҚEncodingпјҢиЁӯзӮәUTF8гҖӮжңҖеҫҢжҚІеҲ°жңҖдёҠйқўпјҢй»һвҖқFinishвҖқжҢүйҲ•пјҢе®ҢжҲҗж–°еўһе°ҲжЎҲзҡ„JDBC Connection PoolгҖӮ е®ҢжҲҗеҫҢпјҢеҸҜд»Ҙй»һж“ҠдёҠең–еҲ—иЎЁзҡ„Connection Poolзҡ„и¶…йҖЈзөҗпјҢйҖІеҲ°дҝ®ж”№з•«йқўпјҢеҸҜй»һж“ҠвҖңPingвҖқжҢүйҲ•пјҢзўәе®ҡжҳҜеҗҰиЁӯе®ҡжӯЈзўәгҖҒиғҪжҲҗеҠҹйҖЈз·ҡгҖӮеңЁй»һж“ҠвҖңPingвҖқжҢүйҲ•д№ӢеүҚи«Ӣе…ҲжҢүз…§2.2.9д№ӢжӯҘй©ҹйҖІиЎҢSQLжӘ”жЎҲеҢҜе…Ҙд»Ҙе»әз«ӢиіҮж–ҷеә«иЎЁе–®гҖӮ ж–°еўһJDBC Resources й»һJDBC Resources й»һNewвҖҰ JNDI NameеЎ«APMDatabaseпјҢPool Nameй»һйҒёAPMPoolпјҢй»һвҖқOKвҖқжҢүйҲ•еҚіеҸҜе®ҢжҲҗж–°еўһAPMзҡ„JDBC ResourcesгҖӮ еҸҜд»ҘзңӢеҲ°APMDatabaseе·Іж–°еўһеҲ°JDBC ResourcesеҲ—иЎЁдәҶгҖӮ

entry.title: APM-RabbitMQ
entry.content: OpenStack RabbitMQ Deep Dive for Integration and MonitoringLink:https://messeiry.com/openstack-rabbitmq-deep-dive/ What is RabbitMQ ? Open source message broker Lightweight and easy to deploy on-premises and in the cloud Can be deployed in distributed and federated configurations to meet high-scale, high-availability requirements Rabbit MQ uses AMQP 0-9-1 protocol (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol); which enables conforming client applications to communicate with conforming messaging middleware brokers. RabbitMQ Message brocker that implement AMQP(Advanced Message Queuing Protocol) AMPQP standardlizes messaging using Producers, Broker and Consumers Messaging increases loose coupling and scalability Publishing: Producer send Message to Exchange and Exchange forward Message to the queue Consuming: Consumer pick up the Message from the queue Guarantee of sending messages to queue and ack from consumer Failure Redilivery scenario 1: Producer doesnвҖҷt get confirm from queue cause dupicated meesages scenario 2: Ack failure (Message remain in the queue) cause a consumer get the message again Message will be sent with routing keys Type of exchange: Fanout: Send the message to all the queue Direct: Send to the queue where routing key=binding key Topic: allows partial match (ex: routing key=red. binding key= red.blue will send the message) (star) can substitute for exactly one word (shape. ) #(hash) can substitute for zero or more word(shape.# ) Header: message header instead of routing key Default(nameless) exchange routing key = queue name if routing key =queue name then send message to the queue Special exchange created by RabbitMQ Compares routing key with queue name Indirectly allows sending directly to queue Core concept of Rabbit Message Queue Producer emits massages to exchange Consumer recieves messages from queue Binding connects an exchange with a queue using binding key Exchange compares routing key with binding key Messages distribution depends on exchange type OpenStack and RabbitMQ OpenStack uses a message queue to coordinate operations and status information among services (еғҸopenstackзҡ„зҘһз¶“) йқһеҗҢжӯҘзҡ„еӮіиЁҠжҒҜж–№ејҸ еҶҚеӮіMessagesжҷӮпјҢзӣҙжҺҘдёҹеҲ°exchangeдёӯе°ұдёҚзҗҶд»–пјҢеңЁеӨ§зі»зөұзҡ„жҷӮеҖҷдёҚз”ЁеғҸеҗҢжӯҘзі»зөұжңғжңүдә’зӣёзӯүprocessе®ҢжҲҗжүҚиғҪз№јзәҢеҹ·иЎҢ An agent-less approach to application-level dependency map discovery using virtual machine inspection (APM-MQ) Application dependency mapping (ADM) creates relationships between interdependent applications.(ex: database and web application) The ADT prototype proposes a method to generate accurate application dependency map by intercepting(ж””жҲӘ) VM execution and introspecting(иҮӘзңҒ) VM state. Why we need a distributed applicationвҖҷs performance management system? -> single nodeвҖҷs performance problem in a distributed system would affect whole distributed systemвҖҷs QoS. Too many Divices, NFV, virtual data center(VDC) dirve the ADT Dependency mapping system arise, since SW problem is harder to dectact than HW problem. Existing Technology Install daemon on machine background process to collect inter-machine TCP traffic log and generate inter-machine dependency (can only generate static DM) Socket Based Application Intercept guest OSвҖҷs at packet sending system call Perform VM introspection to get running thread and TCP connection information. Send this traffic log to server Convert thread based traffic log into inter-thread traffic log in APM (Application Performance Management) server Generate accurate application dependency map from inter-thread traffic logs Thread level execution VM introspection help interception at system call enables quick detection and deployment of changes in the machine Multi-process or multi-thread server and the prototype also produces the trajectory along with an accurate ADM Approach for Message Based Application: The software architecture of enterprise service bus is widely used and its underlined message passing mechanism is message queue Problem in application discovery of queue based application: Advantages of using checksum approach

entry.title: 5G Privacy Security
entry.content: 5G Privacy SecurityChallenges Semantic information attacks (ex: App) дәәйӣ»дҝЎе•Ҷйӣ»и…ҰиіҮиЁҠдёҚе°ҚзӯүпјҢAPP иҰҒжұӮйңҖиҰҒдҪҚзҪ®пјҢдёҚзҹҘйҒ“жӢҝеҺ»е№№еҳӣ Timing attacks йҖҡйҒҺиЈқзҪ®йҒӢз®—зҡ„з”ЁжҷӮдҫҶжҺЁж–·еҮәжүҖдҪҝз”Ёзҡ„йҒӢз®—ж“ҚдҪңпјҢйҖҡйҒҺйҒӢз®—жҷӮй–“жҺЁе®ҡиіҮж–ҷзҡ„е„ІеӯҳиЈқзҪ®пјҢжҲ–иҖ…еҲ©з”ЁйҖҡиЁҠжҷӮй–“е·®йҖІиЎҢиіҮж–ҷз«ҠеҸ– IMSI catching attack (fake BS) 5G have different actors such as virtual mobile network operators (VMNOs), communication service providers (CSPs) пғ synchronization of privacy polices IMSI-Catcher Attack solution in 5G3/4G IMSI IMSIзўјпјҢе…Ёзҗғе”ҜдёҖпјҢе°ұеғҸиә«еҲҶиӯүпјҢеҰӮжһңиҰҒйҖЈжҺҘжҷӮйңҖиҰҒеӨ§е–ҠиҮӘе·ұзҡ„иә«еҲҶиӯү(IMSI)е°ұжңғи®Ҡеҫ—еҫҲе®№жҳ“иў«иҝҪи№Ө еҜҰйҡӣдёҠпјҢIMSIдёҚжңғеңЁжҜҸж¬ЎзҷјиЁҠжҒҜйғҪеӮіпјҢжңғз”ұиҮЁжҷӮзҡ„GUTI/TMSIдҫҶз•¶жҡ«жҷӮиә«еҲҶиӯүпјҢеҸӘжңүеңЁдёӢйқўзҡ„е…©еҖӢжғ…жіҒжңғеӮіиҮӘе·ұзҡ„IMSI з•¶жүӢж©ҹйҖЈеҲ°дёҖеҖӢж–°зҡ„жӯЈеёёз¶Іи·Ҝ жүӢж©ҹжңғе…ҲеӮійӣ»дҝЎзҮҹйҒӢе•ҶеҲҶй…Қзҡ„иҮЁжҷӮиә«д»ҪдҝЎжҒҜGUTI/TMSIзөҰBSи«ӢжұӮжҺҘе…ҘзҮҹйҒӢе•Ҷз¶Іи·ҜпјҢBSйҡЁеҫҢжңғиҪүзҷјзөҰCore Network дёӯзҡ„MMEпјҢдёҰеңЁMMEжҹҘи©ўе°ҚжҮүзңҹеҜҰиә«еҲҶпјҢжүҚеҸҜйҖЈз·ҡгҖӮеҰӮжһңжҹҘдёҚеҲ°пјҢе°ұи«ӢжүӢж©ҹзҷј Identity requestпјҢжӯӨжҷӮжүӢж©ҹе°ұеӮі IMSI(зҷјз”ҹж–јйҖЈжҺҘеҲ°ж–°з¶Іи·Ҝе’ҢroamingеҲ°дёҚеҗҢMMEжңҚеӢҷзҜ„еңҚжҷӮ) з•¶жүӢж©ҹйҖЈеҲ°еҒҮеҹәең°з«ҷ жүӢж©ҹжңғиҮӘеӢ•йҖЈеҲ°иЁҠиҷҹеј·зҡ„BSпјҢйҖҸйҒҺйҖҷжЁЈеҒҮеҹәең°з«ҷеҸҜд»Ҙи·ҹжүӢж©ҹиҰҒIdentity Request пјҢжүӢж©ҹе°ұдёҖзӣҙеӮіIMSI Q:зӮәд»ҖйәјIMSIдёҚеҠ еҜҶеӮіијёе‘ў?ANS:еӣ зӮәеңЁе»әз«Ӣе®үе…ЁйҖЈжҺҘжҷӮпјҢдёҖй–Ӣе§Ӣз¶ІзөЎдёҚзҹҘйҒ“жүӢж©ҹзҡ„иә«еҲҶпјҢиҰҒе…ҲзҹҘйҒ“е®ғжҳҜиӘ°пјҢжүҚй–Ӣе§ӢдәӨжҸӣеҜҶй‘°пјҢж ёеҝғз¶ІеңЁеҠ еҜҶдҝЎжҒҜеүҚиҰҒзўәе®ҡе°Қж–№зҡ„иә«еҲҶжІ’е•ҸйЎҢгҖӮ 5G Solution for IMSI Catcher 5Gжұәе®ҡеј•е…Ҙе…¬й‘°з§Ғй‘°зҡ„ж©ҹеҲ¶пјҢе…¬й‘°з”ЁдҫҶе…¬й–ӢдёҰеҠ еҜҶ(еңЁжүӢж©ҹз«Ҝ)пјҢз§Ғй‘°з”ЁдҫҶдҝқз•ҷдёҰи§ЈеҜҶ(еңЁйӣ»дҝЎе•Ҷз«Ҝ) 5GиЈЎзЁұзӮәSUPIпјҲSUbscription Permanent Identifier)йЎһдјјIMSIпјҢSUCI (SUbscription Concealed Identifierпјүе°ұжҳҜйЎһжҳҜе…¬й‘°еҠ еҜҶйҒҺеҫҢзҡ„IMSI жүӢж©ҹеҗ‘еҹәз«ҷgNBзҷјиө·жҺҘе…Ҙз¶ІзөЎзҡ„и«ӢжұӮпјҢзҷјйҖҒSUCIпјҲеҚіеҠ еҜҶзҡ„SUPIпјүжҲ–жҳҜGUTI gNBж”¶еҲ°дҝЎжҒҜеҫҢпјҢиҪүзҷјиҮіж ёеҝғз¶Ізҡ„SEAFпјҲSEcurity Anchor Functionпјү SEAFж”¶еҲ°дҝЎд»ӨеҫҢи§ЈжһҗеҫҢзңӢжҳҜGUTIйӮ„жҳҜSUCIпјҢиӢҘжҳҜGUTIе°ұеҢ№й…ҚеҲ°е°ҚжҮүзҡ„SUPIпјҢиӢҘзӮәSUCIеүҮдёҚи§ЈеҜҶпјҢз№јзәҢеҗ‘AUSFпјҲAuthentication Server Functionпјүзҷјиө·й‘‘ж¬Ҡз”іи«ӢNausf_UEAuthentication_Authenticate RequestпјҢдёҰж”ңеё¶е°ҚжҮүзҡ„з¶ІзөЎжңҚеӢҷдҝЎжҒҜSN-NameпјҢж–№дҫҝAUSFиӘҝз”Ёе°ҚжҮүй‘‘ж¬Ҡз®—жі•AVпјҲAuthentication VectorпјҢеҢ…еҗ«RAND, AUTN, HXRES*е’ҢK_seafпјү AUSFйҖҡйҒҺеҲҶжһҗSEAFж”ңеё¶зҡ„з¶ІзөЎдҝЎжҒҜSN-NameпјҢзўәе®ҡжүӢж©ҹжҳҜеҗҰеңЁз¶ІзөЎжңҚеӢҷзҜ„еңҚе…§пјҢдёҰдҝқеӯҳжүӢж©ҹйңҖиҰҒзҡ„з¶ІзөЎжңҚеӢҷдҝЎжҒҜпјҢжҺҘдёӢдҫҶз№јзәҢе°ҮSUCIжҲ–SUPIе’ҢжңҚеӢҷз¶ІзөЎдҝЎжҒҜSN-NameиҪүзҷјзөҰUDMпјҲUnified Data Managementпјү еңЁUDMдёӯиӘҝз”ЁSIDF(Subscription identifier de-concealing function)е°ҮSUCIи§ЈеҜҶеҫ—еҲ°SUPIпјҢз„¶еҫҢйҖҡйҒҺSUPIдҫҶй…ҚзҪ®жүӢж©ҹе°ҚжҮүжүҖйңҖзҡ„й‘‘ж¬Ҡз®—жі• жҺҘдёӢдҫҶдҫҝжңғж №ж“ҡжүӢж©ҹзҡ„й‘‘ж¬Ҡж–№ејҸдёҖжӯҘжӯҘжҸҗеҸ–е°ҚжҮүзҡ„й‘‘ж¬Ҡз§ҳй‘°иҲҮй‘‘ж¬ҠзөҗжһңпјҢзӣҙиҮіжңҖеҫҢе°ҮзөҗжһңеҸҚйҘӢзөҰжүӢж©ҹпјҢжүӢж©ҹз«ҜUSIMжңғж Ўй©—з¶ІзөЎеҒҙжүҖзҷјйҖҒй‘‘ж¬Ҡзөҗжһңзҡ„зңҹеҒҪ But still attacker may force UEs to communicate in non-5G mode (Bidding Down Attack)

entry.title: Weihnachtsabenteuer иҒ–иӘ•еҶ’йҡӘиЁҳ
entry.content: Weihnachtsabenteuer2013е№ҙжҲ‘еҸғеҠ дәҶдәӨжҸӣиЁҲз•«еҲ°еҫ·еңӢдәӨжҸӣдёҖе№ҙпјҢ2013е№ҙзҡ„иҒ–иӘ•зҜҖпјҢжҳҜдәәз”ҹдёӯжңҖзү№еҲҘзҡ„иҒ–иӘ•зҜҖпјҢйӮЈе№ҙжҳҜжҲ‘з¬¬дёҖж¬ЎпјҢзңҹжӯЈж„ҹеҸ—еҲ°иҒ–иӘ•дҪізҜҖе®¶ж—ҸеңҳиҒҡзҡ„жә«йҰЁж°Јж°ӣпјҢжӯӨеӨ–пјҢе®ғжӣҙжҳҜдёҖж®өе……ж»ҝеҶ’йҡӘиҲҮй©ҡеҘҮзҡ„ж—ҘеӯҗгҖӮ еңЁжҜҸе№ҙзҡ„иҒ–иӘ•зҜҖеҒҮжңҹеүҚзҡ„дёүеҲ°еӣӣеҖӢйҖұжң«пјҢеҫ·еңӢзҡ„еӨ§еҹҺеёӮйғҪжңғиҲүиҫҰдёҖе№ҙдёҖеәҰзҡ„иҒ–иӘ•еёӮйӣҶпјҢжҲ‘зҡ„еҜ„е®ҝе®¶еәӯзӮәдәҶеё¶жҲ‘й«”й©—иҒ–иӘ•еёӮйӣҶпјҢзү№еҲҘеңЁйӮЈеҖӢе№ҫеҖӢйҖұжң«жҠҠжҷӮй–“з©әдёӢдҫҶпјҢеё¶жҲ‘еҺ»йҷ„иҝ‘еӨ§еҹҺеёӮзҡ„иҒ–иӘ•еёӮйӣҶйҒҠзҺ©пјҢеёӮйӣҶзҡ„зҮҲе…үзӮәеҜ’еҶ·зҡ„еҶ¬еӨңй»һиө·дәҶе№ҫеҲҶзҶұй¬§иҲҮе–§еӣӮпјҢжҲ‘еҖ‘жј«жӯҘеңЁеёӮйӣҶдёӯпјҢдёҖйӮҠйҖӣи‘—иҒ–иӘ•йЈҫе“Ғж”ӨдҪҚпјҢдёҖйӮҠдә«еҸ—и‘—йҖҷзҫҺеҘҪиҲҮе®¶дәәе…ұиҷ•зҡ„жҷӮе…үпјҢиө°зҙҜдәҶгҖҒйӨ“дәҶпјҢе°ұжүҫе№ҫеҖӢзҫҺйЈҹж”ӨдҪҚдёҰеңЁж—ҒйӮҠзҡ„з«ҷжЎҢе°ұеҗғиө·жқұиҘҝдј‘жҒҜиө·дҫҶпјҢе°ұз•¶жҲ‘еҖ‘еҗғзҡ„жӯЈзҲҪзҡ„жҷӮеҖҷ,ж—ҒйӮҠзӘҒз„¶жңүдҪҚеҘіеЈ«еҝ«жӯҘзҡ„иө°еҲ°жҲ‘еҖ‘жЎҢеүҚ,дёҹдёӢдәҶдёҖеҖӢжқұиҘҝ,дёҰеҝ«жӯҘзҡ„йӣўй–ӢдәҶгҖӮжҲ‘дёҖжҷӮжІ’еҸҚжҮүйҒҺдҫҶпјҢд»ҘзӮәжҳҜйӮЈеҖӢеҘіеЈ«еүӣеҗғе®ҢжқұиҘҝиҰҒжҠҠеһғеңҫдёҹеңЁжЎҢдёҠи®“ж”ӨдҪҚзҡ„дәәдҫҶж”¶жӢҫпјҢд»”зҙ°дёҖзңӢ,еҺҹдҫҶжҳҜдёҖжң¬жӣёпјҢжҲ‘иҪҹзҲёзңӢи‘—жҲ‘иӘӘпјҡгҖҢйҖҷжң¬жӣёжҳҜд»–йҖҒзөҰдҪ зҡ„зҰ®зү©,иҰҒеҘҪеҘҪз•ҷи‘—пјҒгҖҚдёҰеҸ«жҲ‘её¶и‘—,еҘҪеҘҪж”¶иө·дҫҶ,жҲ‘жІ’жғіеӨӘеӨҡе°ұеҸӘжҳҜз•¶еҒҡеҶҚеҒҡе–„дәӢе°ұжҠҠд»–ж”¶иө·дҫҶ,з•¶дҪңиҒ–иӘ•еёӮйӣҶзҡ„зҙҖеҝөе“Ғеҗ§гҖӮдәӢеҫҢз•¶жҲ‘зҝ»й–Ӣжӣёжң¬зҡ„з¬¬дёҖй ҒжҷӮ,жӣёдёҠеҜ«и‘—пјҡгҖҢйҖҷжҳҜдёҖжң¬иҮӘз”ұзҡ„жӣё,её¶и‘—д»–дёҖиө·еҺ»ж—…иЎҢеҗ§,еёҢжңӣд»–еҸҜд»ҘйҷӘдҪ еәҰйҒҺдёҖж®өзҫҺеҘҪзҡ„жҷӮе…үпјҢз•¶дҪ и®Җе®ҢжҷӮпјҢиЁҳеҫ—е°ҮйҖҷжң¬жӣёеӮізөҰдёӢдёҖеҖӢдәә,и®“дёӢдёҖеҖӢдәәиғҪеӨ её¶и‘—дҪ зҡ„з¶“й©—,дёҖзӣҙеӮійҒһдёӢеҺ»гҖҚпјҢйҖҷеҖӢжғіжі•зңҹзҡ„жҳҜйқһеёёжңүж„ҸжҖқпјҢж–јжҳҜжҲ‘жұәе®ҡз№јжүҝд»–зҡ„ж„Ҹеҝ—пјҢз№јзәҢи‘—жҲ‘зҡ„еҫ·ж„Ҹеҝ—д№Ӣж—…гҖӮ жҲ‘з№јзәҢиҒ–иӘ•еёӮйӣҶдёӯжј«йҒҠпјҢжңҹеҫ…и‘—дёӢдёҖеҖӢй©ҡеҘҮиҲҮд»ӨжҲ‘зӮәд№Ӣй§җи¶ізҡ„зҡ„дәӢзү©гҖӮеңЁдәәжө·дёӯпјҢжҲ‘еҖ‘зҷјзҸҫдәҶдёҖеҖӢйӯ”иЎ“её«, жҲ‘еҖ‘дёҚзҙ„иҖҢеҗҢзҡ„еңҚи§ҖдәҶйҒҺеҺ»пјҢжӯӨжҷӮйӯ”иЎ“её«йңҖиҰҒдёҖдҪҚе°Ҹе№«жүӢ, жҲ‘зҡ„иҪҹзҲёдёӢж„Ҹиӯҳзҡ„жҠҠжҲ‘жҺЁдәҶеҮәеҺ»пјҢдёҰиҲүиө·дәҶжҲ‘зҡ„жүӢпјҢжӯӨжҷӮзҡ„жҲ‘еҝғдёӯеҸӘжңүиҗ¬еҲҶзҡ„жғ¶жҒҗпјҢеҗ‘дёҠеёқзҘҲзҰұйӯ”иЎ“её«еҚғиҗ¬дёҚиҰҒйҒёеҲ°жҲ‘пјҢеҸҜжҳҜдёҠеёқжІ’жңүй Ҷи‘—жҲ‘зҡ„ж„ҸжҖқпјҢеҸҚиҖҢзөҰжҲ‘дәҶжңҖеҘҪзҡ„е®үжҺ’пјҢжҲ‘еңЁзңҫеӨҡиҲүжүӢзҡ„дәәдёӯиў«йҒёдёӯдәҶпјҢе°ұеҘҪеғҸе‘ҪдёӯжіЁе®ҡдёҖжЁЈгҖӮ ж–јжҳҜжҲ‘дёҚзҹҘе“ӘдҫҶзҡ„еӢҮж°ЈпјҢжҲ‘з«ҷдёҠдәҶеҸ°пјҢз•¶йӯ”иЎ“её«зңӢеҲ°жҲ‘дәһжҙІдәәзҡ„йқўеӯ”жҷӮпјҢд»–и©Ұи‘—з”ЁиӢұж–Үи·ҹжҲ‘еҘ—еҘ—иҝ‘д№ҺпјҢе•ҸжҲ‘еҫһе“ӘеҖӢеңӢе®¶дҫҶпјҢеңЁеҫ·еңӢеҒҡд»ҖйәјпјҢжҲ‘иҮӘдҝЎең°з”Ёеҫ·ж–Үеӣһд»–иӘӘ:гҖҢжҲ‘жҳҜеҸ°зҒЈдәәпјҢжҲ‘дҫҶеҫ·еңӢз•¶дәӨжҸӣеӯёз”ҹгҖҚпјҢе°ҸиҒҠдәҶдёҖдёӢеҫҢпјҢйҖҷе ҙдё–зҙҖе°·е°¬зҡ„йӯ”иЎ“иЎЁжј”е°ұй–Ӣе§ӢдәҶгҖӮ йҰ–е…ҲпјҢд»–еҸ«жҲ‘еҫһж’Іе…ӢзүҢдёӯжҠҪдёҖејөзүҢдёҰиЁҳдҪҸе®ғпјҢжҺҘи‘—д»–е°ҮзүҢйҒһзөҰжҲ‘дёҰеҸ«жҲ‘жҠҠзүҢжҙ—дёҖжҙ—пјҢз„¶еҫҢи·ҹжҲ‘иӘӘ:гҖҢз•¶д»–ж•ёеҲ°дёүжҷӮжҲ‘е°ұжҠҠзүҢдёҹеҲ°з©әдёӯгҖҚпјҢеҗҢжҷӮд»–еҫһи…°йҡӣжҺҸеҮәдёҖжҠҠеҜ¶еҠҚпјҢдёҰиӘӘйҒ“пјҡгҖҢжҲ‘жңғз”ЁйҖҷжҠҠеҠҚдҫҶжүҫеҮәдҪ зҡ„зүҢгҖҚпјҢжҺҘи‘—иӘӘпјҡгҖҢжҲ‘еҖ‘е…Ҳз·ҙзҝ’дёҹдёҖж¬ЎпјҲйҖҷеҸҘи©ұжҲ‘жІ’иҒҪжё…жҘҡпјүгҖҚпјҢж–јжҳҜд»–ж•ёдёҖгҖҒдәҢгҖҒдёүпјҢжҲ‘е°ұе°Үе…ЁйғЁзүҢзҒ‘дәҶеҮәеҺ»пјҢжӯӨжҷӮе…Ёе ҙе“„е ӮеӨ§з¬‘пјҢйӯ”иЎ“её«е°·е°¬зҡ„е°Үең°дёҠзҡ„зүҢе…ЁйғЁж’ҝдәҶиө·дҫҶпјҢдёҰеҶҚдёүи·ҹжҲ‘зўәиӘҚжҲ‘жңүдәҶи§ЈйҒҠжҲІиҰҸеүҮпјҢйҖҷж¬ЎдҫҶзңҹзҡ„, д»–ж•ёдёҖгҖҒдәҢгҖҒдёүпјҢжҲ‘е°Үе…ЁйғЁзҡ„зүҢеҫҖд»–иә«дёҠеҘ®еҠӣдёҖдёҹпјҢд»–жӢҝи‘—еҜ¶еҠҚи©Ұең–иҰҒеҫһзүҢе ҶдёӯеҲәеҲ°жҲ‘йҒёзҡ„йӮЈејөзүҢпјҢдҪҶдәӢиҲҮйЎҳйҒ•пјҢд»–зҡ„еҜ¶еҠҚдёҠз©әз©әеҰӮд№ҹпјҢе…Ёе ҙйғҪиў«йҖҷеҖӢж“ҚдҪңзөҰй©ҡе‘ҶдәҶпјҢйӯ”иЎ“её«е°·е°¬зҡ„еҶҚдёҖж¬Ўж’ҝиө·ең°дёҠзҡ„зүҢпјҢи·ҹжҲ‘иӘӘпјҡгҖҢзӯүдёӢиЁҳеҫ—еҫҖеӨ©з©әдёҠдёҹгҖҚпјҢеҸ«жҲ‘еҶҚдёҹдёҖж¬ЎпјҢдёҖгҖҒдәҢгҖҒдёүпјҢд»–еҲәдёӯдәҶ!йӯ”иЎ“её«зөӮж–јж”¶иө·дәҶе°·е°¬еҸҲдёҚеӨұзҰ®иІҢзҡ„еҫ®з¬‘пјҢдёҰе°ҮеҠҚдёҠзҡ„зүҢйҒһзөҰдәҶжҲ‘еҸ«жҲ‘еӨ§иҒІе–ҠеҮәдҫҶе®ғжҳҜд»ҖйәјгҖӮ е…Ёе ҙеұҸж°ЈеҮқзҘһпјҢе°ұзӮәдәҶиҰӢиӯүйҖҷеҖӢеҘҮи№ҹзҡ„жҷӮеҲ»пјҢз„¶иҖҢж®ҠдёҚзҹҘз•¶дёӢжүҚжҳҜе…Ёе ҙжңҖе°·е°¬зҡ„жҷӮй–“пјҢжҲ‘зңӢи‘—зүҢпјҢзүҢзңӢи‘—жҲ‘пјҢжҷӮй–“еҪ·еҪҝеҮҚзөҗдҪҸдәҶпјҢжҲ‘жҠҪеҲ°зҡ„зўәеҜҰжҳҜй»‘жЎғ4жІ’йҢҜпјҢиҗ¬иҗ¬жІ’жғіеҲ°зҡ„жҳҜпјҢжҲ‘з«ҹз„¶дёҚзҹҘйҒ“й»‘жЎғзҡ„еҫ·ж–ҮпјҢжӯӨжҷӮе ҙйқўи¶ҠеҠ е°·е°¬жҲ‘еҸӘеҘҪеӨ§е–ҠеҮәй»‘жЎғ4зҡ„иӢұж–ҮпјҲ4 of SpadeпјүдёҰеҢҶеҝҷзҡ„и·‘дёӢеҸ°пјҢеҝғдёӯдёҖиҗ¬йҡ»иҚүжіҘйҰ¬йЈӣеҘ”иҖҢйҒҺпјҢзңҹзҡ„жҳҜдёҹиҮүдёҹеҲ°е®¶дәҶгҖӮ еӣһжҶ¶иө·дҫҶпјҢеҳҙи§’зёҪжңғеҫ®еҫ®дёҠжҸҡпјҢжңүдәәиӘӘпјҡгҖҢдәәз”ҹиӢҘдёҚжҳҜдёҖе ҙеҶ’йҡӘпјҢйӮЈе°ұд»Җйәјд№ҹдёҚжҳҜгҖҚпјҢжҲ–иЁұе°ұжҳҜдёҖеҖӢз„Ўеҝғзҡ„жұәе®ҡпјҢжүҖеё¶дҫҶзҡ„й©ҡеҘҮиҲҮдёҚзўәе®ҡпјҢдәәз”ҹзҡ„ж•…дәӢжүҚеҰӮжӯӨиҖҗдәәе°Ӣе‘ігҖӮ

entry.title: еҰӮдҪ•жҲҗзӮәдёҖеҗҚе„Әз§Җзҡ„иЁӯиЁҲеё«
entry.content: еҰӮдҪ•жҲҗзӮәдёҖеҗҚе„Әз§Җзҡ„иЁӯиЁҲеё«: зҫ…еӯҗйӣ„ еҰӮдҪ•е…Ҙй–ҖдёҰжҸҗеҚҮиҮӘе·ұзҡ„иЁӯиЁҲж°ҙе№і? зңӢ:зңӢеӨ§йҮҸе„Әз§Җзҡ„дҪңе“Ғ from(Behance, pintrest, FWA, dribbble) и’җйӣҶдҪңе“Ғ~~ еҰӮжһңдҪ зҷјзҸҫдҪ дёүеҖӢжңҲеүҚи’җйӣҶзҡ„дҪңе“ҒжҳҜеһғеңҫ йӮЈдҪ е°ұйҖІжӯҘдәҶ еҒҡ: еӢ•жүӢеҒҡ еҺ»еҜҰдҪңеӯёзҝ’ иҖҢдёҚжҳҜдёҖзӣҙеӯёе·Ҙе…· жғі:жҖқиҖғзҡ„ж–№ејҸ еҲҘзҡ„иЁӯиЁҲеё«жҖҺйәјеүөдҪңзҡ„? зӮәд»ҖйәјиҰҒйҖҷжЁЈиЁӯиЁҲзҡ„ дёҚеҒңең°е•ҸзӮәд»Җйәј жҜҸеҖӢиЁӯиЁҲиғҢеҫҢйғҪжңүдёҖеҖӢеҺҹеӣ жңүз”ҡйәјз”ҡйәјзҡ„зҠ§зүІеҰҘеҚ” еҰӮжһңйҒҮеҲ°дёҖеҖӢеҫҲеӨ§зҡ„е•ҸйЎҢз„Ўжі•и§Јжұә?жҠҠеӨ§е•ҸйЎҢ еҲҶжҲҗеҫҲеӨҡе°Ҹе•ҸйЎҢ е°Ҹе•ҸйЎҢжІ’иҫҰжі•и§Јжұә еҶҚеҲҶжӣҙе°Ҹзҡ„е•ҸйЎҢ зңӢ2.0 еҲқзҙҡйҡҺж®ө:д»ҝйҖ еҲҘдәәзҡ„иЁӯиЁҲ йҖІйҡҺйҡҺж®ө:зө„еҗҲгҖҒеҖҹй‘‘еҲҘдәәзҡ„иЁӯиЁҲ жғі2.0 Good artist copy, great artist steal еҒҡ2.0 еҖҹй‘‘ зңӢеҲҘдәәдҪңе“Ғзҡ„е„Әй»һ зө„еҗҲ зө„еҗҲдёҚеҗҢжқұиҘҝ ж”№йҖІ жӣҙ_/жӣҙ_ redesign Qoute: е°Қж–јйҖҷдәӣиЁӯиЁҲдёҚж»ҝж„ҸпјҢеҸҜд»ҘйҖҸйҒҺиҮӘе·ұзҡ„ж–№ејҸеҺ»еӯёзҝ’пјҢеҠӘеҠӣж”№е–„еҘ№пјҢд»»дҪ•дәәеҫһе№іеҮЎеҲ°йқһеҮЎйңҖиҰҒдёҚж–·е …жҢҒгҖҒеӨ§йҮҸзҡ„еҠӘеҠӣ еҘҪжҳҜдёҚеӨ зҡ„пјҢеӣ зӮәдҪ еҝ…й ҲжҜ”еҘҪеңЁи·ҹе„Әз§ҖдёҖдәӣпјҢд№ӢжүҖд»ҘйңҖиҰҒеӯёзҝ’

entry.title: еҝғзҡ„еј·еӨ§пјҢжүҚжҳҜзңҹжӯЈзҡ„еј·еӨ§
